* A modern office environment designed to foster productivity, creativity, and teamwork empowering you to bring your best

title: Forensic Accounting- Manager
description:
**Count on us.** Our "we\-care" culture is more than just a motto; it's a promise. From day one, we prioritize your growth, well\-being, and success. You can count on us to support your career journey and help you achieve your professional goals. Join us.


Our management consulting team focuses on our clients’ critical business needs. At Plante Moran, we have a privilege of serving clients and providing consultative services as they face challenges and seek new opportunities. By joining our Forensic and Valuation Services (FVS) team as a Forensic Accountant, you’ll spend your time providing litigation support and performing fraud investigations.


**Job Description:** Responsible for conducting thorough analyses to uncover and document evidence related to various types of financial fraud and litigation disputes. Your expertise will play a crucial role in helping our clients understand and mitigate financial risks.

 **Your role.**


Your work will include, but not be limited to:

* Conduct detailed forensic investigations and financial analyses.
* Collect, organize, and analyze documentation.
* Prepare comprehensive reports which support findings.
* Stay updated with the latest forensic accounting tools and techniques.

 **The qualifications.**

* Licensed Certified Public Accountant (CPA).
* 4\-7 years of experience in forensic accounting or a related role.
* Experience with data analysis software (e.g., Alteryx, Power BI, Tableau, IDEA, Valid8, BankScan).
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
* Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE) preferred.
* Certified in Financial Forensics (CFF) preferred.
* Knowledge of extracting information from commonly used accounting systems.
* Familiarity with using Relativity to search for relevant information within email and hard drive collections.
* Experience performing OSINT research to identify relevant records.


This is an exempt position, so you may have to work hours that exceed the standard 40\-hour work week.

**What makes us different?**


On the surface, we’re one of the nation’s largest audit, tax, consulting, and wealth management firms. But dig a little deeper, and you’ll see what makes us different: we’re a relatively jerk\-free firm (hey, nobody ‘s perfect) with a world\-class culture, consistent recognition as one of Fortune Magazine’s “100 Best Companies to Work For,” and an endless array of opportunities. At Plante Moran, diversity, equity and inclusion means that all staff members have equitable and fair opportunities to succeed, in an inclusive environment, with their individual, unique identities. So, what are you waiting for? Apply now.

*Plante Moran enjoys a “Workplace for Your Day” model which, simply put, means we strive for flexibility and balance while staying true to our principally in\-person model. We believe that face\-to\-face interactions are paramount for individual and collective development, but also encourage individuals to work with their supervisor and team to determine their optimal working environment each day.*

*Plante Moran is committed to a diverse workplace. We strive to create a culture where each person feels accepted and valued. We believe that each person’s ultimate potential begins with first acknowledging their inherent dignity. When we can recognize — and celebrate — our many human differences, we’re able to create a workplace where all staff feel a sense of belonging and an opportunity to succeed. This allows us to attract and retain the best talent, serve clients through diverse thinking, and better represent and support the various communities in which we live and work. Plante Moran is an Equal Opportunity Employer.*

*Plante Moran maintains a drug\-free workplace.*

*Interested applicants must submit their resume for consideration using our applicant tracking system. Due to the high volume of interest in our positions, only candidates selected for interviews will be contacted. Candidates must be legally authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship, with the exception of qualified candidates who are bilingual in either Japanese and English or Chinese and English. Unsolicited resumes from search firms or employment agencies, or similar, will not be paid a fee and become the property of Plante Moran.*

 *The specific statements above are not intended to be all\-inclusive.*


We are pleased to offer eligible staff a robust benefits package. Eligibility and contribution requirements for some of these benefits vary based on the number of hours staff work per week. Highlights include health, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance. In addition to this, eligible staff are able to take advantage of our Flexible Time Off and various pre\-determined holidays, as well as a 401(k) plan, flexible benefits plans, business\-related travel expense, lodging, and meal reimbursement for business\-related use. A pension plan is also available for eligible administrative and paraprofessional staff. A discretionary bonus plan is available for eligible staff. Plante Moran also offers some contingent staff positions the option to elect health insurance in addition to limited paid sick time.


The compensation range reflects the base salary we reasonably expect to pay for this position across our offices in the following regions: CO, IL, OH, and MA. Please review the position description for the applicable geographic location. Generally, experienced hires are not hired at or near the maximum salary range, as compensation decisions take into account a wide variety of factors, including but not limited to: responsibilities, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and geography. For early career roles, including campus hires, we offer standardized, market\-based starting salaries among individuals within the same hiring group. This approach ensures fairness and reflects current industry benchmarks for entry\-level talent.


Under Colorado’s Job Application Fairness Act, you have the right to redact, from any documents that you submit in connection with your application, information that identifies your age, date of birth, or dates of attendance at or graduation from an educational institution. Should you wish to exercise your right to redact such information, please redact it prior to submitting documentation.


The compensation range for this role in CO, IL, OH, and MA is: $120,000\.00\-$145,000\.00

title: Systems Administrator
description:
We need one part\-time Systems Administrator to support the Domain Name Change Preparation Project in the NA timezone. This role keeps BAU running while supporting identity\-focused analysis for the upcoming transition from woven\-planet.global to woven.toyota.

BAU Backfill

* Maintain daily SysAdmin operations so core team members can focus on the project. • Handle identity\-related tasks including account updates, access troubleshooting, and group management. • Support system administration work across Windows, M365, and core IT infrastructure. • Investigate and resolve authentication or account issues. • Keep BAU documentation current.

**Requirements**

* Strong knowledge of Entra ID and identity attributes (UPN, proxy addresses, Object ID).
* Understanding of SAML and OIDC authentication flows. • Experience reviewing how applications match users and how changes affect access.
* Solid troubleshooting across Windows, M365, and enterprise systems.
* Clear communication with technical and non\-technical teams.

**Preferred qualification:**

* Experience with SCIM\-based provisioning.
* Familiarity with SuccessFactors\-driven identity updates.
* Prior involvement in domain or identity transition projects.
* Proficiency in business level Japanese.

Job Type: Part\-time

Pay: $44\.76 \- $53\.90 per hour

Expected hours: 20 per week

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Vision insurance

Application Question(s):

* Do you have hands\-on experience managing Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), specifically working with UPN changes, proxy addresses, Object ID, and identity attribute updates?
* Do you have experience reviewing how applications match users (Object ID vs UPN vs Email) and understanding how changes to those attributes impact authentication?
* Do you have experience with identity provisioning tools such as SCIM and integrations like SuccessFactors syncing user attributes into Entra ID?
* Do you have strong hands\-on experience in Windows Server administration, Microsoft 365, account management, group management, and authentication troubleshooting?
* Are you comfortable working onsite in Palo Alto, CA three days per week for a 20\-hour\-per\-week contract role for 9 months, and collaborating with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders?

title: Service Coordinator/Case Manager - 1 + years experience
description:
**SUMMARY:**

Develops, coordinates and facilitates activities which result in an increased awareness of the services available and rendered to people with developmental disabilities by performing various functions, such as: case consultation, social assessments, resource development, treatment modalities, development of person\-centered plans, individualized family service plans and coordinating family and persons served diagnosis and treatment techniques. Facilitates the education of persons served, families, and service providers regarding available services.

**POSITION SPECIFICATIONS:**

1\. EDUCATION

Master’s degree from an accredited school in Social Work, Human Services, Drug and Alcohol, Education, Counseling, Psychology, Criminal Justice or

Bachelor’s degree from an accredited school in Human Behavioral Science, which would typically include degrees in:

· Child Development

· Counseling

· Psychology

· Social Work

· Sociology

The degree **must** be one of those listed above ***and*****must** include a course of study of a combined total of 30 semesters, or 45 quarter hours, in the Development of Human Behavior, Child Development, Family Intervention Techniques, or Diagnostic Measures of Therapeutic Techniques all of which are typically required for degrees in Child Development, Counseling, Psychology, Social Work and Sociology.

2\. WORK EXPERIENCE:

Minimum of one year case management experience within a social services, health care/service or related environment required.

3\. OTHER SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

a) Ability to communicate effectively verbally and in writing at the highest professional level with families, vendors and service communities.

b) Ability to effectively present information to RCOC resource groups.

c) Participates in internal agency activities, which lead to the development of collaborative efforts between RCOC and other services and supports for people with disabilities.

d) Ability to apply RCOC mission statement and guiding principles towards solving everyday issues.

e) Must be a self\-starter who can work on complex problems, interacting with a wide variety of constituents with priorities.

f) Must exercise skill in creative problem solving minimal supervision and direction.

g) Must be proficient in the use of a computer and independently produce all persons served related reports and correspondence.

4\. CERTIFICATIES/LICENSES/REGISTRATIONS:

proof of valid California driver’s license and auto insurance. Must have a means of transportation that will permit incumbent to discharge job duties effectively and efficiently.

**ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILIES:**

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each of the following essential duties satisfactorily with minimum supervision. Reasonable accommodations may be made to individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

1\. Coordinates initial assessment and development of individualized family service plan, person\-centered plans; coordinates ongoing revision of person\-centered plans.

2\. Participates in various resource groups to improve the quality of life for people with disabilities and their families.

3\. Secures and uses appropriate information, to include psychological, physical, and social factors contributing to the persons served situation, in monitoring and revising the person\-centered plan.

4\. In conjunction with the RCOC resource groups, designs intervention plans, identifies and secures resources to meet the objectives of the individual’s person\-centered plan.

5\. Performs evaluation of services to ensure continuous monitoring for quality of purchased service(s).

6\. Provides opportunities to our persons served for education, support and problem solving in classroom, RCOC sanctioned workshops, as well as individual settings.

7\. Completes necessary documentation in accordance with state and federal regulations, as well as Agency policy and procedures.

8\. Complies with policies, procedures and performance standards and initiatives.

9\. Plans and implements innovative initiatives that expand and clarify the options available to people with disabilities.

10\. Participates, at the discretion of the area manager, mentoring of team members.

11\. In accordance with RCOC policy, implements the highest standards of customer service in all activities.

12\. Acts within the scope of authority in a manner consistent with Agency guidelines, policies and practices.

13\. Works with other team members to coordinate activities necessary to assure effective team function.

title: Portuguese Video Game Tester
description:
**Native Language Reviewer (Gaming)** 

*Does anyone know a* ***native speaker of (Brazilian) Portuguese*** language *who might be interested in paid work reviewing video game content?*

We’re looking for **Native Language Reviewers** to help test and review native\-language text and audio in video games. **No prior gaming, QA, or translation experience is required — training is provided.** 

Join a collaborative team passionate about video games and language quality.

**Who We Are!**

You name it, we’ve done it. GlobalStep partners with the world’s leading game studios and innovative brands, bringing compelling and high impact digital experiences to life, fast and flawless. Our specialized gaming practice has helped leading video game studios launch and support over 3,000 Video Game titles that have generated over $30 billion in game revenue.

GlobalStep provides creative and technical outsourced solutions to the video games industry. Our services span the entire development lifecycle and include Game Design, Art, Engineering, Quality Assurance, Localization, Localization Quality Assurance, and Player Engagement. With 20 years' experience in the games industry, our 2,500\+ employees operate in 30 countries and eight fully integrated, ISO\-certified studios across the Americas, Europe, and Asia. Our clients include all major platform owners, top AAA studios, and hundreds of large and mid\-size developers.

Due to continued expansion in the US , GlobalStep is looking for Localization QA Testers. You will be part of a growing testing team providing technology services to our global customer base.

As a tester, you’ll be responsible for:

* Working on internationally released video games used by millions of players worldwide.
* Ensuring the quality of video game and related software translations, checking the quality of the language used in various texts and audio files on different media.
* Providing a comprehensive localization test for subtitles, localized audio files, scripted text, and more.
* Reporting any type of error in spelling, grammar, syntax, punctuation, and/or translation quality, inconsistency, incorrect terminology into our database. You will suggest appropriate corrections and improvements, to improve the quality of service that is requested by the client.
* Ensuring that the content is well adapted to the (Spanish, French, Chinese, Korean, German, Turkish, Polish, Italian, or Japanese) language, culture and market.

You’ll receive hands\-on training and have opportunities to grow within our QA and localization teams.

**What Will Help You Succeed in This Role:**

* Must be native or fluent in one of the listed languages (spoken and written)
* You understand the culture and everyday usage of the language
* You have a good command of written, spoken and reading English
* You’re comfortable reviewing text and identifying spelling, grammar, or phrasing issues
* You can work independently and collaborate with others when needed
* You are familiar with (if you know how to use it even better!) computer tools such as Excel, Google Sheets, Google Drive and JIRA
* Experience in localization, QA, or translation is a plus, but not required.

title: Accounting Manager - Bachelor's Degree Accounting ONLY
description:
**Summary**

The Accounting Manager is responsible for overseeing all day\-to\-day accounting and finance operations. This role ensures accurate financial reporting, proper inventory accounting, cash management, and internal control execution while supervising a team. The position requires a hands\-on leader who can manage accounting processes efficiently in a streamlined environment and support the Director of Accounting/Finance in reporting to the foreign parent company.

**Essential Duties \& Responsibilities Accounting Management**

* Lead monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes in compliance with U.S. GAAP.
* Ensure accuracy of the general ledger and financial statements (P\&L, balance sheet, cash flow).
* Review journal entries, reconciliations, and supporting schedules.
* Prepare reporting packages for submission to the foreign parent company.
* Maintain proper documentation and audit trail.
* Monitor and manage company cash activity (single bank account environment).
* Perform bank reconciliations and review cash transactions.
* Oversee AP payment scheduling and AR monitoring.
* Prepare basic cash forecasts and support working capital monitoring.
* Support standard costing for a single\-product manufacturing operation.
* Ensure accurate inventory accounting for raw materials, WIP, and finished goods.
* Monitor production\-related costs and perform basic monthly cost variance analysis.
* Ensure proper application of U.S. GAAP inventory capitalization and absorption costing.
* Execute internal control procedures designed by the Director.
* Ensure proper segregation of duties within a small team structure.
* Maintain compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
* Support external audits and coordinate with external CPA firms.

Supervisory Responsibilities

* Supervise and mentor staff members.
* Oversee AP, AR, GL, and inventory accounting functions.
* Establish practical and efficient accounting procedures suitable for a small organization.
* Collaborate with operations and purchasing teams to ensure smooth financial processes.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in accounting.
* 6–8 years of progressive accounting experience.
* Experience coordinating with U.S. Tax Accounting.
* Experience in manufacturing or inventory accounting preferred.
* Strong understanding of U.S. GAAP.
* SAP experience required.
* Prior supervisory experience preferred.
* CPA preferred but not required.
* Comfortable working in a small, hands\-on environment.
* Strong attention to detail.
* Organized and process\-oriented.
* Effective communication skills with operations and headquarters.

**Physical Demands**

* Must be able to respond to visual and audible alarms.
* Regular and consistent attendance at the office
* May require minimal travel to company offices or external meetings, if applicable.
* Prolonged periods of working on a computer.
* Ability to use hands to handle office equipment such as computers, keyboards, calculators, and phones.
* Frequent communication with team members and other departments must be able to exchange accurate information clearly.
* Visual acuity required for reviewing detailed financial reports and working on screens.
* Ability to frequently be stationary and sit for extended periods of time.
* Ability to occasionally stand and walk short distances.
* Ability to lift, carry, push, and pull 15 lbs.
* Ability to lift overhead and at or below shoulder level.
* Ability to enter text or data into a computer or other machine by means of a traditional keyboard.

**Communication**

* Strong verbal, written, and presentation skills.
* Must be able to effectively communicate with all levels of employees.
* Participates in in\-person collaboration, team meetings, and cross\-functional communication.
* Korean and Japanese language speaking and writing ability is a plus.

**Work Environment**

* Full\-time position (typically Monday\-Friday)
* Office / Plant Environment
* Open concept workspace requiring close proximity to team members and other departments.
* Managing tasks with potential background noise and visual distractions
* May require additional hours during peak reporting periods, month and year end close and/or audits.
* Maintaining professionalism, discretion, and confidentiality in a shared environment.
* Ability to work independently and maintain accuracy and attention to detail despite potential interruptions.

**Reports To**

* Director of Accounting/Finance

Note

This job description is not intended to be all\-inclusive. Employee may perform other related duties as needed to meet the ongoing needs of the organization.

description:

 **About the company:**



Located in Redondo Beach, CA, GungHo Online Entertainment America Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of the Japanese game company GungHo Online Entertainment, Inc. Publishing titles across multiple platforms ranging from console to handheld and mobile, our group is comprised of veterans from various aspects of the industry brought together by one simple thing: a love of gaming.

 **Position Summary:**

  


Associate Producer position is responsible for the upkeep of a live operations product and the production period of a package product. Job duties also include maintaining records of a game title, checking for fan feedback, and ensuring the accuracy of the information handled. If you wish, this position may have a path towards becoming a Producer.

 **What you'll be doing:**


* Work as a liaison between the Japanese development teams and GOEA production team for a live operations title.
* Responsible for monitoring product updates and maintenances.
* Request information required for social media and marketing efforts.
* Share fan feedback with Japanese development teams.
* Accurately translate informational material from written Japanese to English and vice versa.
* Provide game assets to promote the game (screenshots, art, video capture).
* Obtain clearance for use of assets for PR usage from the IP holders of the developed title.
* Generate ideas on how to utilize available assets to promote the game via events/tradeshows.
* Work with Community Management to coordinate post\-launch content development.
* Establish and communicate KPI targets.
* Has a grasp on the game project’s contract and coordinates with finance (for making payments post\-milestones, receiving payments post\-launch from platforms).
* Assist working with legal counsel, executives, external developers/vendors on contract negotiation and drafting, and monitor milestone tracking and payment schedules upon contract execution.
* Provide feedback on marketing material.
* Check accuracy of in\-game messaging.
* Check accuracy of messaging on all official channels.
* Ensure correct timings of messages, announcements, and reveals.
* Manage and mentor more junior members when assigned.
* Ensure that company goals are communicated to reports.
* Handle timesheets and other supervisory tasks when assigned.

  


**Qualifications and desired experience:**


* Must have business\-level proficiency in both English and Japanese, written and verbal.
* Bachelor's degree preferred.
* Two to four years of experience in the same or a similar position within the game industry
* Excellent attention to detail.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
* Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
* A proven track record of delivering results in a data\-driven environment is a plus.
* trong public speaking and presentation skills are a plus.
* Flexibility to work overtime as needed.
* Willingness to travel for business as required.
* Passion for games!

title: Marukan Vinegar Controller
description:
**This is not a remote position. In office in Paramount CA**  

  

Position / Job Title: Controller  

Department: Finance \& Administration  

Status: Exempt  

Shift: 8:00 am – 5:00 pm  

Reports to: Chief Operating Officer  

Immediate Supervisor: Chief Operating Officer  

Positions Supervised: Accounting \& Office Manager, Accounting Assistants  

  

**JOB SUMMARY*** FINANCIAL LEADERSHIP, INTEGRITY, AND OPERATIONAL ACCOUNTABILITY
* The Controller serves as the senior accounting leader for the organization, reporting directly to the Chief Operating Officer. This hands\-on role is responsible for the integrity, accuracy, and timeliness of financial reporting while supporting business decision\-making through disciplined financial oversight, including budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning and analysis activities.
* In the absence of a Chief Financial Officer, the Controller ensures strong internal controls, reliable reporting, and practical financial insight to support operational performance. The role oversees the monthly close process, maintains compliance with U.S. GAAP, and serves as the primary financial liaison to Japan headquarters.
* The Controller collaborates closely with operations and department leaders to improve financial visibility, cost management, and reporting efficiency while maintaining a disciplined financial culture across the organization.

**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**  

  

**FINANCIAL LEADERSHIP, INTEGRITY, AND OPERATIONAL ACCOUNTABILITY**  

**General Accounting Oversight*** Direct all accounting functions including accounts payable, accounts receivable, general ledger management, reconciliations, accruals, and cash flow tracking.
* Oversee monthly, quarterly, and annual financial closings, ensuring completeness, timeliness, and accuracy.
* Review and approve financial statements, cash flow reports, and variance analyses.
* Maintain and refine chart of accounts and ledger classifications.

**Budgeting, Forecasting \& Financial Planning*** Lead development and coordination of the annual budget process.•
* Prepare rolling forecasts and perform variance analysis.
* •Analyze revenue trends, COGS, margins, and operating expenses.•
* Develop financial models to support pricing and cost management initiatives.
* Provide forward\-looking insights into cash flow and profitability drivers
* Support scenario planning and operational decision\-making.

**Compliance \& Audit Readiness*** Lead audit preparation and serve as primary liaison with external auditors and tax professionals.
* Ensure all statutory filings (local, state, federal) are accurate and submitted on time.
* Monitor compliance with financial policies and governmental regulations.
* Maintain effective internal controls and segregation of duties.
* Identify financial risks and recommend mitigation strategies

**Headquarters Reporting \& Communication**•* Prepare monthly financial reporting packages for U.S. leadership and Japan headquarters.•
* Participate in recurring financial review meetings.
* Provide clear explanations of financial performance, trends, and key variances.
* Ensure alignment between U.S. GAAP reporting and headquarters reporting requirements

**Team Leadership \& External Partner Oversight*** Provide functional leadership to accounting personnel.
* + Delegate transactional accounting activities while retaining final review authority.
	+ Manage engagements with external accounting outsourcing firms.
	+ Establish clear expectations, task ownership, and communication cadence.

**Systems \& Process Optimization*** **E**valuate accounting procedures for efficiency and accuracy.
* + Recommend and implement improvements to workflows and reporting processes.
	+ Ensure integration of accounting systems with operational platforms.
* Drive automation initiatives to enhance reporting timeliness and accuracy

**Continuous Improvement \& Organizational Alignment*** Promote a culture of transparency, accountability, and fiscal responsibility.
* Align financial operations with organizational KPIs and strategic priorities.
* Support executive leadership with actionable financial insights.

**OTHER DUTIES*** Perform other tasks and special projects as assigned by leadership.
* Adjust work schedule or delegate responsibilities based on operational priorities.


QUALIFICATIONS  

  

**Education/Certification:*** Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent (Required)
* + CPA Certification or equivalent licensure (Preferred)
* Master’s Degree in Accounting, Business Administration, or Finance (a plus)

**Required Knowledge and Skills:*** Strong understanding of GAAP and financial reporting standards
* Experience leading monthly close processes
* Budgeting and forecasting experience
* Strong analytical and strategic thinking capabilities
* Ability to communicate effectively across departments and leadership levels
* Fluency in English and Japanese for professional financial communication
* Commitment to ethical financial stewardship and confidentiality

**Experience:*** Minimum 8–12 years of progressive accounting experience
* Experience in a small to mid\-sized organization preferred
* Experience managing internal staff and/or third\-party service providers
* Experience supporting cross\-border reporting preferred

**PROVISION FOR ABSENCE*** Accounting \& Office Manager

**TRAINING PROVIDED**•* COO
* Supplemented with ongoing professional development support

title: Senior Business Management Specialist
description:
We are seeking a highly experienced individual contributor to serve as a Senior Business Management Specialist. Although this position does not involve managing direct reports, the position carries significant responsibility and requires hands\-on ownership of financial management, corporate governance, and cross\-company coordination with headquarters in Japan.  

  

**Key Responsibilities**:  

Business \& Financial Management:* Own business planning and performance management based on revenue, direct costs, and SG\&A.
* Translate financial plans into operating profit forecasts and track performance through budgeting, forecasting, variance analysis, and reporting.
* Provide practical, data\-driven insights and execute end\-to\-end cost management with strong accountability.


Headquarters Coordination \& Compliance:* Serve as the primary operational counterpart to Japan HQ.
* Lead executive and board\-level coordination, statutory and internal audits, PAM, and other governance requirements.
* Ensure reliable execution of HQ requests while bridging HQ expectations with overseas operational realities.


Cross\-Entity Collaboration \& Process Design:* Support effective collaboration between KSX and parent company KAM.
* Contribute to the design and improvement of evaluation frameworks and operational processes across entities.
* Identify gaps and drive practical, execution\-focused improvements as an individual contributor.


Industrial IoT Go\-to\-Market Planning \& Global Coordination:* Support go\-to\-market planning for industrial IoT connectivity under a CoE model.
* Act as a central hub linking HQ, overseas subsidiaries, and local teams to enable global business expansion.
* Support internal alignment, product standardization, and hands\-on resolution of contract and delivery issues.


Tax \& Regulatory Coordination for Service Delivery:* Ensure appropriate tax treatment for each service, including surcharges and regulatory fees.
* Coordinate with internal teams and external advisors for new services or material changes, and support implementation into billing systems.
* Trigger reassessment when pricing or service scope changes.


Billing Oversight \& Issue Resolution:* Oversee billing accuracy across carriers and partners to protect profitability.
* Investigate discrepancies or overcharges, lead root\-cause analysis, and drive corrections or credits.
* Implement preventive controls to reduce recurrence of billing issues.


Strategic Account Support for a Key Japanese Automotive OEM:* Provide business\-side support for a critical Japanese automotive OEM account.
* Coordinate impact assessments for change requests across cost, timeline, and contracts.
* Support customer communication and contract amendments in close collaboration with sales and legal teams.


Global Marketing Support \& Coordination:* Support global marketing initiatives in collaboration with Japan HQ.
* Plan and execute participation in major global events, ensuring cross\-functional alignment and resource coordination.
* Support post\-event reporting and external communications, including press releases, in line with HQ guidelines.

**Required Qualifications**:* Advanced knowledge of financial management and management accounting, with the ability to manage through operating profit level.
* Proven experience handling headquarters\-driven governance, audits, and compliance processes.
* Strong stakeholder coordination skills across geographies and organizational boundaries.
* Ability to work independently, prioritize effectively, and take full ownership of deliverables.
* Business\-level proficiency in English; Japanese proficiency strongly preferred.

**Preferred Experience**:* Experience at a major Japanese corporation, preferably at a senior specialist or group leader level.
* Experience working with overseas subsidiaries or cross\-border teams.
* Employment Classification.

* Medical, Dental and Vision Coverage


* Basic Life Insurance and AD\&D

* 401(k) with company match

title: Expert Jewellery - Japan
description:
At Catawiki, every day brings the extraordinary! Whether it’s Daniel Ricciardo’s Formula 1 Car, a Woolly Mammoth’s Skeleton, Lady Gaga's Jumpsuit or Usain Bolt’s running shoe, we encounter exceptional objects every day.
  
  

We’re a one\-of\-a\-kind marketplace for buying and selling special objects. Each week, more than 110,000 unique items are auctioned, all carefully curated by our passionate in\-house experts.
  
  

Having sold over 25 million unique objects, our mission is to become the world’s most popular destination for special objects. As a growing, diverse and sustainable scale\-up, we proudly live by three core values. If these values resonate with you, we’d love to explore how you can join us.
  
  

* Taking ownership and driving impact
* Being open to change and feedback
* Being passionate about our mission and our customers.


Tasks
  
  

Your Mission
  
  

As a Jewellery Expert specialising in Pre\-Owned and Brand Jewellery, you drive the quality and growth of Catawiki’s Jewellery auctions in Japan. You combine gemmological expertise with commercial judgement to curate compelling auctions that attract engaged bidders and trusted sellers. Working within the Luxury Department, you safeguard transparency and fairness while strengthening our position in the Japanese jewellery market.
  
  

What You’ll Do
  
  

* Evaluate submitted lots by reviewing titles, descriptions and images, and provide realistic and well\-supported value estimates
* Curate attractive jewellery auctions that balance quality, market demand and commercial potential
* Maintain high standards of transparency and fairness through professional and courteous communication with sellers
* Partner with Sales to recruit and develop new sellers and diversify the selection of lots in your auctions
* Collaborate with Marketing to increase bidder attention and drive engagement for your auctions
* Support dispute resolution by applying expert judgement and acting as a trusted mediator when needed


Requirements
  
  

What You’ll Bring
  
  

* Expert knowledge of pre\-owned and branded jewellery
* Gemmology Diploma from a school recognised by FEEG and GIA
* At least 5 years of experience in the jewellery trade, including manufacturing processes and quality control
* Proven experience in appraising and assessing pre\-owned and brand jewellery
* Strong understanding of market prices and the commercial considerations of curating jewellery for online auctions
* Established network of sellers and experts, with documented commercial or customer\-facing experience
* Fluent English and Japanese language skills, strong communication abilities, solid computer skills and diplomatic judgement in user disputes


Benefits
  
  

Where You’ll Be
  
  

This is a 100% remote role based in Japan.
  
  

Why You'll Love Working with Us
  
  

* Create a visible impact by working at scale in a global organisation serving millions of customers across 80\+ categories. In our flat structure, every role has a broad scope and directly impacts both our customers and the business.
* Learn and grow through our Learning \& Development initiatives, including clear development plans and mentorship programmes to support your career progression.
* A culture of connection defines us. We’re a passionate, diverse team of 800\+ Catawikians representing 60\+ nationalities. We foster an inclusive and queer\-friendly environment where everyone is encouraged to bring their full self to work.
* Celebrate life’s moments with us. You’ll receive a €100 Catavoucher when you join, a €50 Catavoucher on your birthday, and an extra day off each year to “Pursue Your Passion\\\*\\\*”\\\*\\\*. We also offer additional leave for key work anniversaries and important life events. Benefits may vary by location.


Interested?
  
  

Apply with an English CV and Cover Letter. By applying, you agree to Catawiki’s Applicant Privacy Policy. If you’re excited about this role but don’t meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

description:
Restaurant Manager  
G.LION Hawaii – Honolulu, HI  
Full\-Time \| $65,000–$75,000 annually

Overview  
G.LION Hawaii is looking for a Restaurant Manager who leads with heart, humility, and a deep sense of hospitality. Our restaurants welcome guests from all over the world, and we rely on leaders who can bring out the best in their teams while honoring the spirit of aloha in every interaction.

This role is hands\-on and relationship\-driven. You’ll guide the flow of the dining room, support our culinary and beverage teams, and nurture a culture where service feels warm, genuine, and consistent. If you enjoy mentoring others, creating memorable guest moments, and contributing to both classic and contemporary dining environments, we would be glad to meet you.

What You’ll Do

* Oversee daily front\-of\-house operations, ensuring smooth service and thoughtful guest care.
* Work side\-by\-side with chefs and beverage leadership to elevate the experience in each outlet.
* Lead with aloha while coaching, training, and inspiring your team to grow.
* Support scheduling, staff development, and clear communication across departments.
* Build lasting guest relationships through warmth, professionalism, and attention to detail.
* Assist with financial responsibilities including planning, forecasting, and operational review.
* Maintain standards of food quality, safety, and cleanliness.

Who You Are  
We’re looking for someone who thrives in a lively, guest\-focused environment and brings equal parts empathy and accountability. You should enjoy connecting with others, problem\-solving in real time, and upholding high standards in both service and professionalism.

Preferred Qualifications

* Previous restaurant management experience

title: Product Development Coordinator/Admin
description:

Inaba Foods is a manufacturer of premium pet food, treats, toppers, and complements, deeply rooted in a family tradition of excellence that spans generations. Since 1958, pet parents have trusted Inaba to deliver products that are human\-grade, nutrient\-rich, and designed to bring joy to pets.


Today, Inaba is the most popular brand of premium cat and dog dinners and treats in Japan, with growing distribution throughout the United States and globally. * Other duties as assigned

* Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Food Science, or a related field preferred
* 3 years of experience in product development, marketing, or project coordination
* Detail\-oriented with strong proofreading and organizational skills
* Excellent written and verbal communication
* Bilingual English/Japanese **REQUIRED**
* Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines in a fast\-paced environment
* Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word)
* Experience working with or knowledge of AAFCO labeling standards is a plus
* Comfortable working with external vendors, consultants, and designers
* Self\-motivated, collaborative, and able to take initiative

title: Sr. Solution Architect (Project Hire)
description:
### **JOB SUMMARY:**


The Senior Solutions Architect’s role is to strategically design global application architectures that support multi\-park needs while also satisfying the tailored needs of individual parks. This Senior Architect will work closely with application owners and other solution architects to design integrations with security, performance, reliability, reusability, scalability, and maintainability in mind. Individual will also be involved in the design of new systems and critical platform and software applications including cloud technologies. Maintain an in\-depth technical knowledge across Universal’s global portfolio of systems and platforms and act as a subject matter expert for other architects, delivery teams and support teams.


In addition, the Senior Solutions Architect will provide technical guidance over the implementation phase of solutions with cross functional Agile teams across the organization including recognizing risk, working with vendors, and communicating with senior leadership. The Sr. Solutions Architect is also responsible for providing technical guidance and providing technical mentorship to junior members of the team.


This Architect will collaborate with cross functional teams to deliver and finalize solutions. This includes, Product, Project Management, IT Security, Infrastructure, Data Architecture and Delivery teams. As a go\-to Sr. Architect, this person will be a subject matter expert across domains and will influence the strategic direction the organization is driving to and will possess excellent communication and collaboration skills to serve as an advisory member of technical direction.


### **MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES:**


* Strategy \& Planning – Act as visionary and strategist for their solution domain area. Assist, design and implement long\-term strategic goals and short\-term tactical plans for managing and maintaining corporate technology and software. Ensure that proposed and existing solution architectures are aligned with Data Architecture and Enterprise Architecture. Provide architectural expertise, and direction to delivery teams, DevOps engineers, other architects. Develop, document, and communicate plans for investing in solutions architecture, including analysis of cost reduction opportunities. Conduct research on emerging technologies in support of systems development efforts and recommend technologies that will increase cost effectiveness and systems flexibility. Provide technical assessments for new work efforts. Lead development of formalized solution methodologies. Build and maintain repository for deliverables and methodologies.
* Solution Design – Where applicable, architect, design/develop, and oversee implementation of Enterprise solutions. Be a domain expert for development teams as part of implementation. Document and maintain the organizations existing solutions architecture and technology portfolio; influence strategic direction and deliver the ‘art of the possible’ for improvements and/or alternatives. Review and provide guidance on new and existing solutions design delivered by other Architects with an eye for compliance, industry standards and architectural SOPs. Interface and coordinate tasks with internal and external technical resources. Collaborate with Project Managers, vendors and IT stakeholders to provision estimates and develop overall implementation plan for installation, customization, and integration efforts. Model business and systems processes based on findings through use case scenarios, sequence diagrams, swaggers, workflow diagrams, and data models.
* Technical Collaboration – Confer with delivery teams, product team, or senior management to refine business requirements and collaborate on technical designs and resolve technical issues. Participate and actively engage with other architects and domain experts at the solution review board.
* Understands and actively participates in Environmental, Health \& Safety responsibilities by following established UO policy, procedures, training and team member involvement activities.
* Performs other duties as assigned.


### **EDUCATION:**


* Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Systems Engineering or similar essential required.


### **EXPERIENCE:**


* 9\+ years of IT experience with large integrated projects; or equivalent combination of education and experience.
* Extensive experience in developing strategic systems architecture. Proven experience in system design and development. Proven experience in system and network design and development.
* Repeatedly demonstrated ability to produce requirement artifacts: data flow diagrams, UML modeling techniques include sequence, component, deployment and domain models.
* An expert level background in software development with ability to code at some level and knowledge of APIs and SDKs.
* A technical architecture background with an understanding of high\-level networks, routers, switches, firewalls, BGP, caching, SANs, servers, and operating systems form a part of the solution stack.
* Strong experience with reverse proxies, web servers, API gateways, service\-oriented architectures, web services (REST), microservice patterns, service buses and integration patterns, security practices, database technologies.
* Proven experience with databases and data storage including NoSQL and SQL databases, along with in memory key value stores.
* Demonstrable experience in solution architecture
* Demonstrable experience in integration solution design


### **ADDITIONAL INFORMATION:**


This individual will understand vision, communicate possibilities of technology solutions along with roadmaps to achieving the results. Experience leading global enterprise engineering projects or teams is a plus. Familiarity with REST, Node.Js, .NET, NoSQL, SQL, native mobile applications, eventing patterns and microservices. Self\-starter with the ability to manage a broad workload and accommodate change with a proven ability to deliver results. Japanese language experience a plus.


Your talent, skills and experience will be rewarded with a competitive compensation package.

title: Cash Management Sales - Director
description:
SMBC Group is a top\-tier global financial group. Headquartered in Tokyo and with a 400\-year history, SMBC Group offers a diverse range of financial services, including banking, leasing, securities, credit cards, and consumer finance. The Group has more than 130 offices and 80,000 employees worldwide in nearly 40 countries. Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Inc. (SMFG) is the holding company of SMBC Group, which is one of the three largest banking groups in Japan. SMFG’s shares trade on the Tokyo, Nagoya, and New York (NYSE: SMFG) stock exchanges.

  

In the Americas, SMBC Group has a presence in the US, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, and Peru. Backed by the capital strength of SMBC Group and the value of its relationships in Asia, the Group offers a range of commercial and investment banking services to its corporate, institutional, and municipal clients. It connects a diverse client base to local markets and the organization’s extensive global network. The Group’s operating companies in the Americas include Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. (SMBC), SMBC Nikko Securities America, Inc., SMBC Capital Markets, Inc., SMBC MANUBANK, JRI America, Inc., SMBC Leasing and Finance, Inc., Banco Sumitomo Mitsui Brasileiro S.A., and Sumitomo Mitsui Finance and Leasing Co., Ltd.

  

The anticipated salary range for this role is between $152,000\.00 and $200,000\.00\. The specific salary offered to an applicant will be based on their individual qualifications, experiences, and an analysis of the current compensation paid in their geography and the market for similar roles at the time of hire. The role may also be eligible for an annual discretionary incentive award. In addition to cash compensation, SMBC offers a competitive portfolio of benefits to its employees.

**Role Description**
--------------------


Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation (SMBC) seeks a Director for its US Cash Management Sales team, responsible for leading or participating in identifying and marketing treasury services opportunities, building relationships and collaborating with Relationship Managers, and meeting origination targets. The role requires a strong knowledge of Cash Management products, customized solutioning, client relationship building, content preparation, and tracking key performance indicators. Key responsibilities include identifying opportunities, developing tailored solutions, understanding market dynamics, preparing client materials, communicating complex issues, and maintaining pipelines and client relationships. Candidates should have around 8 years of relevant experience, strong analytical and communication skills, proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint, and a collaborative mindset, with Japanese language skills preferred but not required.

**Role Objectives: Delivery**
-----------------------------

* Exhibit strong knowledge in Cash Management / Treasury Services products and capabilities
* Lead and/or participate in identifying opportunities and marketing US Cash Management / Treasury Services products and services, interacting daily and building relationships with clients and Relationship Managers
* Experience in identifying opportunities and customizing solutions for clients based on strong understanding of client industry, best practices, and client priorities
* Lead and/or support the origination, negotiation, and execution of Cash Management / Treasury Services solutions
* Experience in developing a strong understanding of client industry, business, best practices, and client priorities to tailor solutions
* Understand Cash Management market dynamics and industry developments
* Prepare content and customized client material for meetings
* Communicate complex issues and solutions effectively
* Understand and communicate a clear, compelling product vision for SMBC’s Cash Management solutions aligned with business leadership
* Translate business value and user feedback into actionable product developments
* Achieve origination targets and maintain KPI tracking and reporting
* Maintain database on calls and pipeline to review deal flow, deal status, potential opportunities, and monitor client relationships
* Develop relationships across the organization and products, for example exhibiting an understanding of the relationship between Cash Management / Treasury Services and Trade Finance
* Follow general market conditions and trends
**Qualifications and Skills**
-----------------------------

* Recommended years of experience: 8
* Advanced knowledge and understanding of Cash Management / Treasury Services concepts and products, including payments, receivables, liquidity, and fraud management
* Ability to identify opportunities and solutions based on client need and priorities
* Strong analytical skills
* Proactive, collaborative, startup mindset
* Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
* Strong Excel and Powerpoint skills with the ability to customize information based on need and opportunity
* Language preference, but not a requirement: Japanese

**Additional Requirements**
---------------------------


SMBC’s employees participate in a Hybrid workforce model that provides employees with an opportunity to work from home, as well as, from an SMBC office. SMBC requires that employees live within a reasonable commuting distance of their office location. Prospective candidates will learn more about their specific hybrid work schedule during their interview process. Hybrid work may not be permitted for certain roles, including, for example, certain FINRA\-registered roles for which in\-office attendance for the entire workweek is required.

  

SMBC provides reasonable accommodations during candidacy for applicants with disabilities consistent with applicable federal, state, and local law. If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application process, please let us know at accommodations@smbcgroup.com.

title: HR People Operations Partner - Recruiter
description:
**Overview**

The People Operations Partner supports the full employee lifecycle with a primary focus on high volume recruitment for our restaurants. This role combines hands\-on recruiting, on\-site hiring support, HR operations, and ongoing partnership with Unit Managers.

The ideal candidate has restaurant or hospitality operations experience and understands the realities of openings, market\-based hiring challenges, and front\-line staffing needs.

**Essential Duties**

**Talent Acquisitions \& On\-Site Hiring Support**

* Lead full\-cycle recruitment for hourly, supervisory, and management roles across multiple locations.
* Visit units to conduct on\-site hiring events, interviews, and community recruiting outreach.
* Build strong pipelines for high\-turnover and new\-store opening roles.
* Partner with Unit Managers to understand staffing needs and ensure decisions align with

**Company hiring standards.**

* Create job postings, screen candidates, conduct interviews, and coordinate hiring decisions.
* Track recruiting metrics (time\-to\-fill, source effectiveness, offer acceptance rate).
* Work with local universities, community organizations, and job fairs to strengthen talent pipelines.
* Support store openings by coordinating hiring, orientation, and onboarding.

**Onboarding \& HR Operations**

* Coordinate pre\-employment steps including background checks, I\-9 verification, and onboarding documentation.
* Support and occasionally lead new\-hire orientation sessions.
* Maintain accurate HRIS records and assist with employee file compliance.
* Collaborate with payroll and benefits teams to resolve employee questions.
* Assist with scheduling, onboarding logistics, and new store support.

**People Support \& Policy Guidance**

* Serve as a point of contact for Unit Managers on HR processes, hiring best practices, and

employee life cycle questions.

* Provide guidance on consistent interview practices, documentation, and employment law basics (wage \& hour, meal/rest periods, non\-discrimination, etc.).
* Serve as a point of contact for employee relations issues for the units.

Provides additional People Operations and/or HR support as needed, including flexible assistance across the department based on business needs.

**Required Skills and Qualifications**

* Strong communication and customer service skills, with the ability to work effectively with frontline staff, managers, and senior leadership.
* Bilingual in Japanese and English.
* Experience in restaurant operations, hospitality management, or multi\-unit operations.
* Strong experience with fast\-paced, high\-volume hiring environments.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience in Japanese restaurants or the food wholesale industry.
* Experience supporting new store openings or pre\-openings activities.
* Prior multi\-location or franchised operations experience.

**Work Environment**

* This role includes visits to restaurant locations, including kitchen areas and active service environments
* Travel within Southern California is required; domestic travel may be needed depending on business needs.

**Physical Demands**

* Standing and walking for extended periods during restaurant visits.
* Light physical activity such as moving small items or demonstrating procedures.
* Reasonable accommodation can be provided as needed.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $79,040\.00 \- $97,760\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Sales Director
description:
The Sales Director will secure and grow DMP NA’s revenue within prescribed territory. Responsibilities include meeting or exceeding sales targets, potentially managing other sales associates, providing a high\-level of customer support, developing quotations, negotiating terms \& conditions, sales planning, assembling market intelligence, and overall owning the sales life\-cycle for accounts within the territory; including marketing, communications, A/R, product positioning, timing, and business development. DMP NA is in a state of early growth and duties will evolve to include new responsibilities. Successful sales management will lead to career growth opportunities.  

**Main Duties and Responsibilities**  

  

* **Customer Interface**
* + The Sales Director is responsible for the success or failure of the Accounts under management.
* Provide top\-to\-bottom customer support from pre\-sales to end\-of\-service for multiple accounts.
	+ Promptly address and resolve customer\-related issues as they arise with minimal management or staff support.
	+ Negotiate complex and non\-standard commercial and business\-related issues with minimal management or staff support.
	+ Be the Customer Process Owner: be aware of all relevant customer policies, procedures, priorities and trends.
* Serve as an effective liaison between DMP NA and its clients. A successful Sales Director communicates Customer and DMP NA requirements and objectives clearly, efficiently, and has the drive and leadership skills to make progress for both companies.
* Effective at using automotive industry problem\-solving skills to deftly manage unexpected and complex matters.
* Most of all, the Sales Director, must develop effective, credible, and reliable relationships with clients and all internal stakeholders through honesty, skills, individual effort, teamwork, and ownership.

  

* **Grow Revenue**

* A successful Sales Director (S.D.) consistently delivers sales results annually, notwithstanding adverse conditions.
* Develop and/or react quickly to new opportunities,
* Lead the DMP NA team internally to present logical proposals on time and close deals.
* The S.D. learns DMP NA’s technologies and business, and can effectively communicate interests and requirements quickly, accurately, completely and with credibility.
* The S.D. listens to client engineering and purchasing for all issues and interests, deduces the unsaid, anticipates client needs and interests, communicates these to the DMP NA team,
* The S.D. must be able to work on the urgent as well as ahead of current demands to help DMP NA remain competitive.
* Create and present opportunities for new business using all the tools if necessary: strategy/logic, business case justifications, communications, relationships, intuition, emotion, technology trends, etc.
* Develop aftersales and service proposals to extend revenue with existing accounts beyond the established service period.
* Quickly interpret technology differences, internal cost, and market/customer information to develop pricing \& proposed terms and conditions.
* Leverage experience to negotiate customer terms \& conditions.
* Works alertly to the timeframe and proactively drives deal and open issue closure.
  

* **Account control**
* + Meets or exceeds business revenue targets within the territory.
* Develops and maintains account plans.
* Performs solid sales planning: forecasting, variance analysis, opportunity management, and total support of DMP NA’s ISO\-certified sales process.
* Contract management: quickly and thoroughly review contracts, develop positions, negotiate redlines, and take all appropriate actions in advance of agreement and/or contract expiration.
* Expert command of written and spoken English as well as MS Excel


* **Teamwork**

title: Event Division Lead
description:
**Position Summary**


As the Event Division Lead, you will oversee the planning, production, and execution of high\-impact entertainment events for Japanese, U.S., and global clients.


Based in Los Angeles, you will support the Event Division Manager while providing cross\-functional oversight for multiple event projects. This division is responsible for producing panels, exhibition booths, screenings, and brand activations at major conventions and international venues.


We are seeking a hands\-on producer who also possesses strategic leadership capabilities. The ideal candidate has deep experience in event production, strong vendor and client management skills, and a genuine passion for anime, games, and entertainment IPs. You will serve as the bridge between creative and operations, collaborating closely with PR, Social Media Marketing, Creative, and Business Development (BD) divisions.

 **Requirements**  

  

1\. Project Leadership \& Execution* Lead project\-level execution based on the division goals and service standards defined by the Event Division Manager.
* Manage budget and cost control for assigned projects, ensuring financial efficiency.
* Utilize existing department workflows and guidelines, providing proactive suggestions for improvement as needed.
* Oversee staffing plans and vendor rosters to ensure scalability for peak event seasons.
* Provide operational direction and growth support to project team members (producers and coordinators).
* Represent the division in client presentations and inter\-departmental planning meetings.


2\. Event Planning \& Creative Development* Translate client objectives into strategic event plans that maximize fan engagement and brand impact.
* Lead creative concept development for panels, booths, premieres, and launch events.
* Supervise the creation of proposals, budget estimates, and project timelines.
* Align event strategy with PR and digital campaigns to ensure seamless cross\-channel integration.


3\. Panel / Stage Event Production (Execution Leadership)* Direct run\-of\-show, stage direction, and technical coordination (A/V, lighting, FX).
* Oversee rehearsals, talent coordination (voice actors, creators, influencers, MCs), and on\-site operations.
* Manage interpreter assignments and coordinate press access with the PR Division.
* Partner with the Social Media Marketing Division for real\-time reporting and fan interaction.
* Ensure brand consistency, safety, and strict schedule adherence during live execution.


4\. Booth Planning \& Production* Oversee creative concepting, spatial design, traffic flow, and fabrication.
* Supervise booth construction, installation, and teardown in collaboration with fabricators and logistics vendors.
* Integrate experiential elements (photo ops, AR stations, giveaways, demo areas) to enhance fan engagement.
* Collaborate with the Creative Division on visual design and motion assets.
* Coordinate on\-site PR activities and social media activations.
* Manage safety protocols, staff briefings, and daily on\-site operations.


5\. Launch / Premiere Event Management* Plan and execute screenings, premieres, and collaboration activations.
* Manage venue selection, permit applications, guest logistics, red\-carpet setup, and press coverage.
* Ensure cohesive storytelling through event themes, staging, and visual identity.


6\. Team \& Vendor Management* Coordinate collaboration with external partners and freelancers based on project requirements.
* Build and maintain domestic and international vendor networks, including contract and cost negotiations.
* Monitor vendor progress for assigned projects to ensure quality and timely delivery.
* Facilitate project status meetings and cross\-functional syncs as needed.


7\. Cross\-Department Collaboration \& Client Relations* Partner with the PR Division for press relations and earned media opportunities.
* Coordinate with the Social Media Marketing Division on content plans and live coverage.
* Work with the Creative Division on design direction and asset production.
* Support the Business Development Division in pitching, budgeting, and defining the scope of work (SOW).
* Maintain transparent and proactive communication with clients throughout all project phases.


8\. Post\-Event Analysis \& Continuous Improvement* Deliver comprehensive event reports including attendance, media reach, social metrics, and ROI.
* Lead internal post\-mortem sessions to identify lessons learned and improve future processes.
* Manage the archive of creative assets, event photos/videos, and operational documentation.
* Stay informed on industry trends and emerging technologies to enhance future event experiences.


QualificationsRequired:* Proven experience in planning, producing, and managing panels and booths at convention events.
* Deep knowledge of U.S. event logistics, vendor management, permit processes, and safety regulations.
* Demonstrated track record of leading execution and coordinating multiple stakeholders to deliver successful events.
* Strong financial sense with experience in project\-based budget management and cost control.
* Exceptional organizational skills and a strong client\-service mindset.
* Business\-level English fluency (Required); Japanese proficiency is a plus.
* Ability to travel domestically and internationally as required by projects.


Preferred:* Eagerness to build and expand a growing division within an international agency.
* A strong passion for anime, games, film, and pop culture.
* Ability to remain calm under high pressure and lead a team effectively.
* A balance of leadership and flexible problem\-solving skills.
* Strong communication skills to work smoothly across U.S., Japanese, and international teams.
* High creative thinking ability to conceptualize attention\-grabbing events.
* Experience in stage production, spatial design, or video production is a plus.

  

**Benefits**  

A number of programs cover all employees in the manner prescribed by law. Additionally, 33 USA Inc. provides several Company\-designated benefit programs.

* Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental \& Vision)
* Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick \& Public Holidays)
* Hybrid Work Model
* + While we currently offer this hybrid arrangement, please note that our work model may evolve to meet changing business needs. The company reserves the right to modify work arrangements at its sole discretion. We appreciate your understanding and flexibility as we continually assess and optimize our work environment.

title: Brand Ambassador
description:
**NOW HIRING: Brand Ambassador \- Waikk**
=========================================

**Part\-Time \| $20/hr \| Evening Role \| Immediate Start**


Do you light up when you talk to people?  

Are you confident, polished, and naturally engaging?  

Do you thrive in fast\-paced, high\-energy environments?


If so, this opportunity was made for you.


We are hiring a **Brand Ambassador** to represent an exciting live entertainment experience in Waikk. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys being around visitors, loves the hospitality industry, and knows how to create genuine, memorable interactions.


You won't just be standing around \- you'll be actively creating excitement, building interest, and helping guests discover an unforgettable experience.

**Position Overview**
---------------------


As a Brand Ambassador, you are the face and first impression of the experience. Your energy, professionalism, and communication skills directly impact guest engagement and overall attendance.


You will work in a lively, high\-foot\-traffic environment where confidence and enthusiasm matter. This role requires someone who can maintain strong presence and composure in busy and sometimes loud surroundings.

**Position Details**
--------------------

* **Location:** Waikk
* **Start Date:** ASAP
* **Schedule:** Wednesdays \- Sundays (with occasional schedule adjustments based on show calendar)
* **Hours:** 5:00 PM \- 9:00 PM
* **Pay:** $20 per hour ( Weekly pay)
* **Parking:** Parking pass provided (TBC)


This is a consistent part\-time evening role, ideal for someone looking to supplement income while working in a fun and dynamic atmosphere.

**What You'll Be Doing**
------------------------

* Actively engaging with visitors and guests in a warm, welcoming manner
* Generating excitement and interest about the show
* Providing genuine, informed recommendations
* Answering questions confidently and professionally
* Maintaining a polished, professional appearance
* Representing the brand with pride and enthusiasm
* Creating positive interactions that encourage ticket purchases


To ensure authenticity, selected candidates will be invited to attend the show at least once prior to starting. This allows you to confidently and genuinely speak about the experience.

**Who You Are**
---------------


You are:

* Outgoing and naturally friendly
* Comfortable initiating conversations with strangers
* Confident speaking in busy environments
* Polished in appearance and demeanor
* Professional and reliable
* Able to maintain energy throughout your shift
* Strong in verbal communication

**Fluency in Japanese is a significant advantage**, as many guests are international visitors.


Previous experience as a brand ambassador, promoter, hospitality host, or customer\-facing representative is a plus \- but personality, presence, and professionalism are most important.

**Dress Code**
--------------

* Branded t\-shirt provided
* Black pants required
* Closed\-toe shoes required
* Custom costume may be introduced at a later date


You must present yourself in a polished and professional manner at all times.

**Why You'll Love This Role**
-----------------------------

* Work in the heart of Waikk's vibrant atmosphere
* Be part of an exciting entertainment environment
* Meet visitors from around the world
* Gain brand representation and promotional experience
* Consistent evening hours
* Professional yet energetic team culture


If you enjoy hospitality, entertainment, or guest engagement, this is a unique opportunity to build experience in a high\-visibility role.

**How to Apply**
----------------


Ready to bring the energy?


Apply online at: **AlohaHP.com**  

Call or Text: **(808\) 735\-7788**


We're looking for someone who can represent with confidence, engage with authenticity, and bring positive energy every shift.


If that sounds like you \- apply today.

title: Position Title: Regulatory Affairs Manager (Bilingual: Japanese/English)
description:
**Position Overview**

The Regulatory Affairs Manager will build and maintain OPFA’s regulatory systems to ensure full compliance with U.S. and Japanese regulatory authorities. This role supports a diverse portfolio that includes IV solutions, drug products (CFR 210/211\), biologics, APIs, combination products, and medical devices.

You will collaborate closely with internal teams, global colleagues in Japan, and external partners such as CDMOs/CMOs. This position is both strategic and hands‑on—perfect for someone who enjoys creating processes, solving problems, and serving as OPFA’s regulatory expert.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Regulatory Strategy \& Execution**

* Develop and execute regulatory submission strategies for new and existing products.
* Prepare, maintain, and manage **NDA**, **ANDA**, **510(k)**, **BLA**, and other required submissions.
* Serve as OPFA’s primary point of contact with U.S. FDA (CDER, CDRH, CBER).
* Coordinate regulatory activities with Japanese teams; support translation and review of package inserts, labeling, and submission documents (English ↔ Japanese).
* Draft and update U.S. drug labeling and ensure alignment with FDA requirements.
* Monitor regulatory changes in the U.S. and Japan and communicate impacts to leadership and project teams.

**Cross\-Functional \& Global Collaboration**

* Partner with OPF\-Japan, OPFA departments, and global Otsuka entities to ensure alignment with global regulatory expectations.
* Work directly with CDMOs/CMOs to support product development, data collection, and submission readiness.
* Facilitate efficient bilingual communication across teams and help bridge cultural and regulatory expectations.

**Process Development \& Operational Excellence**

* Build scalable RA processes suitable for a growing organization.
* Develop structures for flexible resourcing (contractors, part\-time, FTE).
* Estimate regulatory budgets and forecast submission\-related costs.
* Maintain documentation and ensure audit readiness.

**Required Skills \& Abilities**

* Strong knowledge of U.S. FDA regulatory frameworks; experience with Japanese regulatory agencies highly valued.
* Clear and effective communicator in **both English and Japanese** (verbal and written).
* Skilled at presenting complex regulatory topics to technical and non‑technical audiences.
* Able to lead productive meetings virtually and onsite with cross\-functional teams.
* Adaptable, resourceful, and comfortable in a fast‑paced startup environment.
* High degree of integrity, confidentiality, and professionalism.
* Strong analytical, organizational, and problem‑solving abilities.
* Emotionally intelligent and capable of influencing across multiple levels.

**Minimum Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in a scientific discipline required; advanced degree preferred.
* Minimum **5 years of U.S. FDA regulatory experience**.
* Experience supporting submissions for pharmaceuticals, biologics, or medical devices.
* Bilingual Japanese/English proficiency strongly preferred due to daily interactions with Japan HQ.
* Authorized to work in the United States.

**Work Style \& Physical Requirements**

* Work onsite in Schaumburg, IL or Austin, TX.
* Occasional domestic travel and international travel (primarily Japan).
* Must be able to attend early‑morning or late‑evening meetings to align with Japan time zones.
* Able to lift up to 20 lbs occasionally (documents, samples, etc.).
