NVIDIA is widely regarded as one of the most desirable employers in technology. It leads in High\-Performance Computing, Artificial Intelligence, and Visualization. Our invention, the GPU, acts as the visual cortex of modern computers and powers our products. GPU deep learning sparked modern AI, the next computing era. The GPU serves as the brain for computers, robots, autonomous cars, and conversational AI that understand the world. Today, we are known as “the AI computing company.” We want to grow and hire the smartest people. Join us at the forefront of technology.
NVIDIA is hiring Senior Deep Learning Scientists interested in streaming multimodal conversational AI, including speech, audio, vision, voice chat, and action, as well as human\-AI interaction. You will demonstrate foundational expertise in deep learning, reinforcement learning, computational statistics, and applied mathematics. You will have a chance to define core algorithmic improvements and scale your ideas through our Nemotron platform. You will work on high\-impact, high\-visibility large language model products that improve the experience for millions of users. If you are creative and passionate about real\-world conversational AI issues, come join our Nemotron LLM team. For more details on Nemotron LLM, check https://www.nvidia.com/en\-us/ai\-data\-science/foundation\-models/nemotron/
What you’ll be doing:* Develop, Train, Fine\-tune, and Deploy streaming large language models to power multimodal conversational AI systems encompassing multimodal understanding, speech synthesis, speech\-to\-speech conversation, video generation, UI and animation rendering and control, environment interaction, and dialog reasoning and tool systems
* Apply brand\-new fundamental and applied research to develop products for multimodal conversational artificial intelligence
* Apply techniques such as instruction tuning and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), reinforcement learning with verifiable reward (RLVR), and parameter\-efficient finetuning methods like p\-tuning, adapters, and LoRA. These methods improve embodied conversational LLMs for multiple use cases.
* Lead the collection, development, and labeling of domain\-specific datasets to train LLMs for various multimodal tasks and applications
* Measure and benchmark model and application performance. Analyze model accuracy and bias and recommend the next course of action \& improvements. Collaborate with various teams on new product features and improvements of existing products
* Participate in developing and reviewing code, building documents, and conducting use case reviews and test plan reviews. Help innovate, identify problems, recommend solutions, and perform triage in a collaborative team environment


What we need to see:* Master’s degree (or equivalent experience) or PhD in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, or Applied Math with 8\+ years of experience
* Excellent programming skills in Python with strong fundamentals in programming, optimizations, and software development
* Strong knowledge of ML/DL techniques, algorithms, and tools with exposure to CNN, RNN (LSTM), Transformers (ViT, BERT, BART, GPT/T5, Megatron, LLMs, MoEs)
* Experience with training real\-time audio language, streaming visual language, and streaming real\-time audio\-visual language models, and ViT, BERT, GPT, and Nemotron Models for different computer vision, NLP, and dialog system tasks using “PyTorch” Deep Learning Frameworks and performing data wrangling, tokenization, and multimodal alignment
* Practical experience in natural language processing, speech/audio processing, computer vision, machine learning, and human\-AI interaction
* Hands\-on experience on conversational AI Technologies like Natural Language Understanding, Natural Language Generation, Dialog systems (including system integration, state tracking, and action prediction), Information retrieval, Question and Answering, Machine Translation, etc.
* Understanding of model development life cycle and experience with model development workflows \& traceability, and versioning of datasets, including know\-how of database management and queries (in SQL, MongoDB, etc.).
* Strong collaborative and interpersonal skills, specifically a proven ability to effectively guide and influence within a dynamic matrix environment


Ways to stand out from the crowd:* Native or near\-native fluency is required in one of these non\-English languages: Spanish, Mandarin, German, Japanese, Russian, French, UK English, Arabic, Korean, Italian, or Portuguese.
* Verified background in building LLMs that incorporate knowledge discovery along with reasoning abilities, including disambiguation, clarification, anticipation, and effective error handling for embodied AI systems
* Validated experience adapting LLMs to different domains such as gaming, virtual assistants, video conferencing, and so on
* Contributing experience in integrating embodied AI systems with various sensor inputs (camera, microphone, torch, and so on) and backend action fulfillment systems
* Experience with long\-term reasoning for embodied AI tasks (navigation, mobile manipulation, instruction following, and collaboration with humans) in gaming/physical environments, given natural\-language instructions.


With competitive salaries and a generous benefits package, NVIDIA is considered one of the technology world’s most desirable employers. We have some of the most forward\-thinking and hardworking individuals in the industry working for us. Due to unprecedented growth, our exclusive engineering teams are expanding rapidly.
If you're a creative and autonomous engineer with a genuine passion for technology, we want to hear from you!
Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The base salary range is 184,000 USD \- 287,500 USD.
You will also be eligible for equity and benefits.
Applications for this job will be accepted at least until March 8, 2026\.
This posting is for an existing vacancy.

title: Expert Jewellery - Japan
description:
At Catawiki, every day brings the extraordinary! Whether it’s Daniel Ricciardo’s Formula 1 Car, a Woolly Mammoth’s Skeleton, Lady Gaga's Jumpsuit or Usain Bolt’s running shoe, we encounter exceptional objects every day.
  
  

We’re a one\-of\-a\-kind marketplace for buying and selling special objects. Each week, more than 110,000 unique items are auctioned, all carefully curated by our passionate in\-house experts.
  
  

Having sold over 25 million unique objects, our mission is to become the world’s most popular destination for special objects. As a growing, diverse and sustainable scale\-up, we proudly live by three core values. If these values resonate with you, we’d love to explore how you can join us.
  
  

* Taking ownership and driving impact
* Being open to change and feedback
* Being passionate about our mission and our customers.


Tasks
  
  

Your Mission
  
  

As a Jewellery Expert specialising in Pre\-Owned and Brand Jewellery, you drive the quality and growth of Catawiki’s Jewellery auctions in Japan. You combine gemmological expertise with commercial judgement to curate compelling auctions that attract engaged bidders and trusted sellers. Working within the Luxury Department, you safeguard transparency and fairness while strengthening our position in the Japanese jewellery market.
  
  

What You’ll Do
  
  

* Evaluate submitted lots by reviewing titles, descriptions and images, and provide realistic and well\-supported value estimates
* Curate attractive jewellery auctions that balance quality, market demand and commercial potential
* Maintain high standards of transparency and fairness through professional and courteous communication with sellers
* Partner with Sales to recruit and develop new sellers and diversify the selection of lots in your auctions
* Collaborate with Marketing to increase bidder attention and drive engagement for your auctions
* Support dispute resolution by applying expert judgement and acting as a trusted mediator when needed


Requirements
  
  

What You’ll Bring
  
  

* Expert knowledge of pre\-owned and branded jewellery
* Gemmology Diploma from a school recognised by FEEG and GIA
* At least 5 years of experience in the jewellery trade, including manufacturing processes and quality control
* Proven experience in appraising and assessing pre\-owned and brand jewellery
* Strong understanding of market prices and the commercial considerations of curating jewellery for online auctions
* Established network of sellers and experts, with documented commercial or customer\-facing experience
* Fluent English and Japanese language skills, strong communication abilities, solid computer skills and diplomatic judgement in user disputes


Benefits
  
  

Where You’ll Be
  
  

This is a 100% remote role based in Japan.
  
  

Why You'll Love Working with Us
  
  

* Create a visible impact by working at scale in a global organisation serving millions of customers across 80\+ categories. In our flat structure, every role has a broad scope and directly impacts both our customers and the business.
* Learn and grow through our Learning \& Development initiatives, including clear development plans and mentorship programmes to support your career progression.
* A culture of connection defines us. We’re a passionate, diverse team of 800\+ Catawikians representing 60\+ nationalities. We foster an inclusive and queer\-friendly environment where everyone is encouraged to bring their full self to work.
* Celebrate life’s moments with us. You’ll receive a €100 Catavoucher when you join, a €50 Catavoucher on your birthday, and an extra day off each year to “Pursue Your Passion\\\*\\\*”\\\*\\\*. We also offer additional leave for key work anniversaries and important life events. Benefits may vary by location.


Interested?
  
  

Apply with an English CV and Cover Letter. By applying, you agree to Catawiki’s Applicant Privacy Policy. If you’re excited about this role but don’t meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

title: Cash Management Client Service - Associate
description:
SMBC Group is a top\-tier global financial group. Headquartered in Tokyo and with a 400\-year history, SMBC Group offers a diverse range of financial services, including banking, leasing, securities, credit cards, and consumer finance. The Group has more than 130 offices and 80,000 employees worldwide in nearly 40 countries. Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Inc. (SMFG) is the holding company of SMBC Group, which is one of the three largest banking groups in Japan. SMFG’s shares trade on the Tokyo, Nagoya, and New York (NYSE: SMFG) stock exchanges.

  

In the Americas, SMBC Group has a presence in the US, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, and Peru. Backed by the capital strength of SMBC Group and the value of its relationships in Asia, the Group offers a range of commercial and investment banking services to its corporate, institutional, and municipal clients. It connects a diverse client base to local markets and the organization’s extensive global network. The Group’s operating companies in the Americas include Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. (SMBC), SMBC Nikko Securities America, Inc., SMBC Capital Markets, Inc., SMBC MANUBANK, JRI America, Inc., SMBC Leasing and Finance, Inc., Banco Sumitomo Mitsui Brasileiro S.A., and Sumitomo Mitsui Finance and Leasing Co., Ltd.

  

The anticipated salary range for this role is between $80,000\.00 and $105,000\.00\. The specific salary offered to an applicant will be based on their individual qualifications, experiences, and an analysis of the current compensation paid in their geography and the market for similar roles at the time of hire. The role may also be eligible for an annual discretionary incentive award. In addition to cash compensation, SMBC offers a competitive portfolio of benefits to its employees.

**Role Description**
--------------------


Responsible for addressing client inquiries and actively support cross\-selling efforts by identifying client needs and suggesting solutions through introductions. Performs a variety of client service functions associated with supporting the strategic initiatives of the company to enable business development, revenue growth, client retention and growth, and sales delivery enhancements.

**Role Objectives**
-------------------

**Associate**

**Key Responsibilities**

**Client Support \& Issue Resolution**

* Serve as a primary point of contact for day\-to\-day client inquiries related to cash management services, including payments, and digital banking access
* Resolve service issues by investigating transactions, escalating problems, and coordinating with internal teams as needed
* Troubleshoot client challenges related to ACH, wires, online banking portals, positive pay, remote deposit, lockbox, and liquidity tools
* Ensure timely follow\-up and provide clear, professional communication to clients

**Operational Support \& Service Delivery**

* Process requests entitlement changes, documentation updates, and service modifications
* Monitor key service metrics such as exception items, payment failures, and fraud alerts, ensuring proactive outreach to clients
* Assist with onboarding activities including initial client setup, platform training, and documentation coordination
* Maintain accurate records of client interactions in service\-tracking systems

**Client Engagement \& Relationship Management**

* Build strong working relationships with clients and internal partners, supporting retention and client satisfaction
* Participate in client calls, training sessions, and service reviews to ensure clients are fully educated on available tools and capabilities
* Identify opportunities to improve client experience and promote digital adoption of treasury platforms

**Continuous Improvement**

* Contribute to service process enhancements, FAQ development, and best practices documentation
* Stay current on product updates, system changes, and new cash management capabilities
* Assist senior team members with projects aimed at improving service efficiency and workflow optimization
**Qualifications and Skills**
-----------------------------

* 2–3 years of experience in client service, banking operations, cash management, or treasury support
* Foundational understanding of cash management products such as ACH, wires, RDC, positive pay, lockbox, and digital banking platforms
* Strong problem\-solving skills with the ability to research issues and provide prompt resolution
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills
* Ability to multitask and manage competing priorities in a high\-volume service environment
* Experience working directly with corporate clients preferred
* Japanese language preferred but not required

SMBC’s employees participate in a Hybrid workforce model that provides employees with an opportunity to work from home, as well as, from an SMBC office. SMBC requires that employees live within a reasonable commuting distance of their office location. Prospective candidates will learn more about their specific hybrid work schedule during their interview process. Hybrid work may not be permitted for certain roles, including, for example, certain FINRA\-registered roles for which in\-office attendance for the entire workweek is required.

  

SMBC provides reasonable accommodations during candidacy for applicants with disabilities consistent with applicable federal, state, and local law. If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application process, please let us know at accommodations@smbcgroup.com.

The Vice President, Onboarding \& Implementation Specialist plays a critical role in delivering a seamless, timely, and high\-quality onboarding experience for corporate and institutional clients utilizing cash management and treasury services. This role manages moderate\-to\-high complexity implementations, leads cross\-functional coordination, and ensures clients are fully enabled on the bank’s cash management platforms and products.

* Contribute to documentation improvements, playbooks, and standardized templates
* Maintain up to date knowledge of treasury products, industry changes, and platform enhancements
**Qualifications and Skills**

* Strong understanding of cash management products such as ACH, wires, lockbox, liquidity solutions, RTP, fraud mitigation tools, and digital banking platforms

The position is based in Naples, FL and does require travel. Join our talented team at a global medical device company focused on Helping Surgeons Treat Their Patients Better™.*

  


**Essential Duties and Responsibilities:**


* Participates in overall product strategy, research and development requirements, development and manufacturing coordination for new and emerging products.
* Provides User Needs from their assigned geographic Region of responsibility to Product Management for new/existing product(s).
* Identifies new and strategic market\-specific product opportunities in conjunction with Marketing, Engineering and Sales Management personnel.
* Coordinates with various department functions such as but not limited to: Engineering, Product Management, International Regional Managers, Regulatory Affairs, Logistics, Operations, Demand Planning, Supply Chain, Customer Service, Marketing Communications, Compliance, Med Ed, and Finance to assure successful and profitable product lifecycle.
* Works closely with Group Manager, product team and regional sales teams to develop and manage promotional planning and execution for products and product brands at Arthrex.
* Develops product launch plans for new products in regions in conjunction with US and in\-market product management. Launch plans to include but not limited to; key messaging, marketing materials, training activities, internal and external research and exhibit strategy.
* Supports international downstream marketing initiatives
* Creates and executes international strategic marketing plans.
* Manages the product lifecycle for a specified product segment across assigned Region
* Strategizes with regional sales management on country\-by\-country product approval submissions.
* Provides continuing product surveillance and management of established product lines
* Adapts products messaging and collateral materials to the needs of the assigned Region
* Expands regional and local product expertise through communication with regional product managers through deep dive reports, dynamic bilateral communication, exchange of key data and market intelligence.
* Attends international meetings/congresses and reports any competitive observations such as new product launches, competitive outcome studies and surgeon presentations.
* Helps identify market potential (potential sales, pricing, gap analysis,) through data mining and analysis.
* Assists in Regulatory Affairs related inquiries, priorities and resolves bottlenecks based in business imperatives.
* Identifies third\-party partnering opportunities for complimentary products to broaden product line.
* May travel up to 30% (coordinated and prioritized between International Group Manager, Strategic and Regional Vice Presidents and International Regional Managers).
* Must be able to work with cadaveric specimens.
* In\-office, Naples FL\-based position; not remote.

  


**Education and Experience:**


* Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in business, marketing, engineering or clinical. Master’s degree is preferred.
* Associate level requires at least 1 year of product management and/or sales and/or marketing experience, preferably in orthopedics OR a Master’s degree.
* Product Marketing Manager level requires at least 5 years of experience in medical device product management (up and/or downstream marketing), marketing or an orthopedics sales position required.
* International medical device product management knowledge and experience preferred.
* Expert knowledge in one of the following fields is preferred.
	+ Equipment for Arthroscopy surgery (Pump, Shaver, RF)
	+ Medical Endoscopic Video Systems
	+ Arthroscopy / Sports Medicine
	+ General Surgery, (OBGYN, ENT, HPB)
  


**Knowledge and Skill Requirements/Specialized Courses and/or Training:**


* Excellent written and verbal English communication skills
* Proficient in one other language preferred (Spanish, Mandarin, Japanese and/or Portuguese preferred).
* Skilled in negotiation and collaborating with various functional units to achieve desired business results.
* Effective project management skills.
* Excellent presentation skills.
* Adaptability: flexible, open to new ideas and approaches.
* Excellent listening and problem solving skills, including the ability to identify root cause, customers concerns or objections.

 **Arthrex Benefits**
* Medical, Dental and Vision Insurance
* Company\-Provided Life Insurance
* Voluntary Life Insurance
* Flexible Spending Account (FSA)
* Supplemental Insurance Plans (Accident, Cancer, Hospital, Critical Illness)

title: Freelance Japanese Translator
description:
**Overview**  
Join our dynamic team as a Freelance Japanese Translator and unleash your linguistic talents to bridge cultures and connect ideas across borders! This exciting role offers the opportunity to work remotely, translating a variety of content from Japanese to English and vice versa. You will play a vital part in ensuring clear, accurate, and culturally appropriate communication for diverse projects. If you’re passionate about languages, meticulous in your work, and eager to collaborate with global clients, this position is perfect for you! All engagements are paid, emphasizing the value we place on your skills and expertise.

We have a need for a Japanese Interpreter March 16 2026 for Immigration interview time 10am must be able to get to Bethpage NY.

**Responsibilities**

* Translate written content from Japanese to English and English to Japanese with precision and cultural sensitivity
* Proofread and edit translations to ensure clarity, accuracy, and consistency across all materials
* Interpret spoken language during virtual meetings or live events when necessary
* Transcribe audio or video recordings in both languages, maintaining original tone and context
* Review and revise translations based on feedback to meet project standards and client expectations
* Collaborate with project managers and clients to understand specific language nuances and requirements
* Maintain organized records of all translation projects, terminology databases, and reference materials

**Skills**

* Multilingual proficiency in Japanese and English; bilingual fluency required
* Strong translation skills with a keen eye for detail and cultural nuances
* Excellent communication skills to effectively convey ideas across languages
* Proven experience in proofreading, interpretation, transcription, and linguistic analysis
* Deep understanding of linguistics principles relevant to translation work
* Exceptional writing skills with the ability to adapt tone and style for different audiences
* Prior linguist experience demonstrating versatility across various content types such as technical, marketing, or literary materials

Embark on a rewarding journey where your language expertise makes a meaningful impact! We value your dedication to quality communication and offer a flexible freelance environment that recognizes your talents. Join us in connecting worlds through language!

Pay: $26\.42 \- $45\.00 per hour

**Your role.**

* Solid technical proficiency including knowledge of MS Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook).
* Ability to be in the office five days/week.


This is a non\-exempt position, so you may have to work hours that exceed the standard 40\-hour work week.

**What makes us different?**


On the surface, we’re one of the nation’s largest audit, tax, consulting, and wealth management firms. But dig a little deeper, and you’ll see what makes us different: we’re a relatively jerk\-free firm (hey, nobody ‘s perfect) with a world\-class culture, consistent recognition as one of Fortune Magazine’s “100 Best Companies to Work For,” and an endless array of opportunities. At Plante Moran, diversity, equity and inclusion means that all staff members have equitable and fair opportunities to succeed, in an inclusive environment, with their individual, unique identities. So, what are you waiting for? Apply now.

*Plante Moran enjoys a “Workplace for Your Day” model which, simply put, means we strive for flexibility and balance while staying true to our principally in\-person model. We believe that face\-to\-face interactions are paramount for individual and collective development, but also encourage individuals to work with their supervisor and team to determine their optimal working environment each day.*

*Plante Moran is committed to a diverse workplace. We strive to create a culture where each person feels accepted and valued. We believe that each person’s ultimate potential begins with first acknowledging their inherent dignity. When we can recognize — and celebrate — our many human differences, we’re able to create a workplace where all staff feel a sense of belonging and an opportunity to succeed. This allows us to attract and retain the best talent, serve clients through diverse thinking, and better represent and support the various communities in which we live and work. Plante Moran is an Equal Opportunity Employer.*

*Plante Moran maintains a drug\-free workplace.*

*Interested applicants must submit their resume for consideration using our applicant tracking system. Due to the high volume of interest in our positions, only candidates selected for interviews will be contacted. Candidates must be legally authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship, with the exception of qualified candidates who are bilingual in either Japanese and English or Chinese and English. Unsolicited resumes from search firms or employment agencies, or similar, will not be paid a fee and become the property of Plante Moran.*

 *The specific statements above are not intended to be all\-inclusive.*


We are pleased to offer eligible staff a robust benefits package. Eligibility and contribution requirements for some of these benefits vary based on the number of hours staff work per week. Highlights include health, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance. In addition to this, eligible staff are able to take advantage of our Flexible Time Off and various pre\-determined holidays, as well as a 401(k) plan, flexible benefits plans, business\-related travel expense, lodging, and meal reimbursement for business\-related use. A pension plan is also available for eligible administrative and paraprofessional staff. A discretionary bonus plan is available for eligible staff. Plante Moran also offers some contingent staff positions the option to elect health insurance in addition to limited paid sick time.


The compensation range reflects the base salary we reasonably expect to pay for this position across our offices in the following regions: CO, IL, OH, and MA. Please review the position description for the applicable geographic location. Generally, experienced hires are not hired at or near the maximum salary range, as compensation decisions take into account a wide variety of factors, including but not limited to: responsibilities, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and geography. For early career roles, including campus hires, we offer standardized, market\-based starting salaries among individuals within the same hiring group. This approach ensures fairness and reflects current industry benchmarks for entry\-level talent.

Should you wish to exercise your right to redact such information, please redact it prior to submitting documentation.

This is a non\-exempt position, so you may have to work hours that exceed the standard 40\-hour work week.

**What makes us different?**


On the surface, we’re one of the nation’s largest audit, tax, consulting, and wealth management firms. But dig a little deeper, and you’ll see what makes us different: we’re a relatively jerk\-free firm (hey, nobody ‘s perfect) with a world\-class culture, consistent recognition as one of Fortune Magazine’s “100 Best Companies to Work For,” and an endless array of opportunities. At Plante Moran, diversity, equity and inclusion means that all staff members have equitable and fair opportunities to succeed, in an inclusive environment, with their individual, unique identities. So, what are you waiting for? Apply now.

*Plante Moran enjoys a “Workplace for Your Day” model which, simply put, means we strive for flexibility and balance while staying true to our principally in\-person model. We believe that face\-to\-face interactions are paramount for individual and collective development, but also encourage individuals to work with their supervisor and team to determine their optimal working environment each day.*

*Plante Moran is committed to a diverse workplace. We strive to create a culture where each person feels accepted and valued. We believe that each person’s ultimate potential begins with first acknowledging their inherent dignity. When we can recognize — and celebrate — our many human differences, we’re able to create a workplace where all staff feel a sense of belonging and an opportunity to succeed. This allows us to attract and retain the best talent, serve clients through diverse thinking, and better represent and support the various communities in which we live and work. Plante Moran is an Equal Opportunity Employer.*

*Plante Moran maintains a drug\-free workplace.*

*Interested applicants must submit their resume for consideration using our applicant tracking system. Due to the high volume of interest in our positions, only candidates selected for interviews will be contacted. Candidates must be legally authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship, with the exception of qualified candidates who are bilingual in either Japanese and English or Chinese and English. Unsolicited resumes from search firms or employment agencies, or similar, will not be paid a fee and become the property of Plante Moran.*

 *The specific statements above are not intended to be all\-inclusive.*


We are pleased to offer eligible staff a robust benefits package. Eligibility and contribution requirements for some of these benefits vary based on the number of hours staff work per week. Highlights include health, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance. In addition to this, eligible staff are able to take advantage of our Flexible Time Off and various pre\-determined holidays, as well as a 401(k) plan, flexible benefits plans, business\-related travel expense, lodging, and meal reimbursement for business\-related use. A pension plan is also available for eligible administrative and paraprofessional staff. A discretionary bonus plan is available for eligible staff. Plante Moran also offers some contingent staff positions the option to elect health insurance in addition to limited paid sick time.


The compensation range reflects the base salary we reasonably expect to pay for this position across our offices in the following regions: CO, IL, OH, and MA. Please review the position description for the applicable geographic location. Generally, experienced hires are not hired at or near the maximum salary range, as compensation decisions take into account a wide variety of factors, including but not limited to: responsibilities, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and geography. For early career roles, including campus hires, we offer standardized, market\-based starting salaries among individuals within the same hiring group. This approach ensures fairness and reflects current industry benchmarks for entry\-level talent.

title: Field Account Executive — Automotive Fastening Solutions
description:
**Field Account Executive — Automotive Fastening Solutions**
============================================================


Picture a week where you split your time between the road and the office: you’re visiting OEM and Tier suppliers, gathering new application needs, and following up with precise quotes. Back at your desk, you’re negotiating pricing, sending product information and samples, and coordinating closely with our Rocknel factory team to shepherd new launches through to mass production—solving questions on delivery, quality, and technical details along the way.


**What you’ll do**
------------------


* Drive outside sales through in\-person meetings, phone calls, mail, and email to respond to customer requests.
* Prepare and deliver quotations; provide product information and samples as needed.
* Identify emerging customer requirements and surface new product opportunities.
* Partner with customers and the Rocknel factory from new product launch timing through mass production to ensure smooth transitions.
* Address inquiries on pricing, delivery dates, quality, and technical matters; collaborate with factory teams to resolve issues.
* Communicate routinely with our head office in Japan to align on customer needs and production plans.

**What you bring**
------------------


* 2–5 years of experience working within a Japanese company environment.
* Preferred: outside sales background in the automotive and fastener sectors; familiarity with the automotive industry is a plus.
* Strong reliability, work ethic, and the ability to prioritize, organize, and complete tasks efficiently.
* Proficiency with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and related tools.
* Clear, effective verbal communication skills.
* Japanese conversational ability preferred (not required).
* Capacity to switch quickly between tasks with minimal errors or omissions.
* Commitment to following all OSHA and company safety guidelines.

* Must wear Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) which is required in designated areas.
* Must be able to travel. This job is 60% office, 40% travel for customers.

title: Concierge
description:
**About the Role \& Team**
--------------------------


Every day at Disney Cruise Line we take pride in bringing the magic of Disney to life. We find joy in creating cherished memories and form genuine connections with our guests. We hold each other to the highest degree and always act responsibly while ensuring the safety of fellow crew and guests. United by our Disney values, we work toward excellence in all we do. As a part of our team, you can live and work in a diverse and inclusive environment amidst a professional and supportive community. If you are ready to create unforgettable experiences and grow as a person and as a professional, apply today!  

  

As Shipboard Concierge Manager, you will be responsible for ensuring outstanding customer service, while improving the passenger experience by coordinating activities, excursions, and events during their voyage.  

  

You will report to: Manager, Concierge (shipboard)  

  

Level: 2 Stripes  

  

**Responsibilities :**  

**How You Will Make a Difference**
----------------------------------

* Service designated Concierge Guests by providing personalized itinerary planning, acquiring special bookings, planning special events, assisting with folio settlement or any other special requests that may arise (i.e. spa or dining reservations)
* Deliver the highest level of personalized service and actively interact with Concierge Guests in 32 to 154 staterooms (dependent on vessel) daily, showcasing integrity and efficiency
* Take full ownership of the Concierge Lounge ensuring all food and beverage aspects are well run and implemented. Monitor cleanliness and maintenance of the area ensuring it is up to show quality standards
* Be instrumental in crafting an environment and performing service that is second to none ensuring all Guest requests are implemented in a timely manner

  

**Basic Qualifications :**  

**What You Will Bring to the Team**
-----------------------------------

* Two year hospitality experience with a luxury brand required
* Languages preferred – English, Spanish, French, Portuguese, Mandarin, Japanese, Hindi, Arabic, Tagalog, Bahasa, Malay
* Leadership and concierge experience preferred with knowledge of proper Concierge etiquette
* Resort or Cruise line Concierge experience desired of affluent clientele
* Must be creative and enjoy crafting a fun and safe atmosphere for children
* Clearly convey information
* Proven record to deliver excellent service
* Self motivated, able to work autonomously
* Ability to build relationships amongst other departments

  

**Preferred Qualifications:**  

**How You Will Stand Out**
--------------------------

* Prior ship experience

  

**Additional Information :**  

**This is a** **SHIPBOARD** **role.**
**You must:**
---------------------------------------------------

* Be genuinely interested in a career at sea and willing to live and work onboard a Disney Cruise Line vessel
* Be willing to follow and perform safety role, emergency responsibilities, and associated responsibilities as specified in the ship Assembly Plan
* Be willing to uphold the general safety management responsibilities as specified in the Safety Management System in areas and operations under their control
* If applicable, be willing to share a confined cabin with other crew members and appreciative of working and living in a multicultural environment that has strict rules and regulations

Your Responsibilities:
* Have a valid passport and C1/D Seaman's visa (DCL will provide you with documents to obtain this)
* Complete a pre\-employment medical
* Obtain a criminal background check
* Bring approved work shoes

**\*\******Disney Cruise Line*** **is a drug\-free workplace. All new hires are required to undergo drug/alcohol testing within the first week onboard and throughout their contracts. Failure to pass the drug/alcohol testing will result in immediate termination.**

Logistics and Ordering Control

Responsible for creating monthly usage reports, tracking all incoming shipments, and making updates / new entries to the master data in our ERP system. Analyzes customer forecasts to determine appropriate safety stock levels.

* Use SAP Business One ERP system to track and control orders and inventory.

Must have good communication skills and appropriate English language skills for a business environment.

Critical skills: Teamwork, flexibility, organized, leadership skills, planning, problem solving.

#### **Job Summary:**


Come and join the magic with Aulani, A Disney Resort and Spa! Perks and benefits may include:

* 100% full coverage of healthcare for you and your eligible dependents
* Tuition paid upfront at network schools
* Free lunch
* Free parking
* Free theme park admission and much more!

Poolside Service will have you working on all types of terrain \- sand included so our Guests can continue to enjoy the offerings at Aulani, A Disney Resort \& Spa. Our Cast Members deliver appetizers, salads, burgers, flatbreads, and hand crafted cocktails.

Expect to work in a high Guest service setting where initiating and holding conversations with Guests will be required.

* Must meet state requirements to obtain Hawaii Liquor Commission card

* Must be comfortable cash handling
* Will work around water
* Possess excellent hospitality skills
* Ability to multi\-task and work in a very fast\-paced environment
* Excellent memorization skills
* Speed, accuracy and efficiency are required, as well as the ability to work well under pressure
* Demonstrated ability to own and resolve guest situations
* Self\-directed (can complete tasks with minimal supervision)
* Willing to follow instructions \& take direction
* Flexible/adaptable
* Supportive of other cast working on your team
* Can complete repetitious tasks while maintaining quality
* Strong verbal communication skills
* An understanding of the pivot system \- a seating and order\-taking method


#### **Preferred Qualifications:**


* Previous serving experience
* Previous restaurant experience; understanding of full service and quick service environment
* Previous outdoor work experience preferred
* Familiarity with local ingredients and the classic cuisine of the Hawaiian Islands
* Knowledge of Hawaiian/Japanese language preferred


#### **Additional Information:**


**SCHEDULE AVAILABILITY:**  

Full Time \- Full availability is required seven (7\) days per week, including early mornings, late nights, weekends, and holidays.

title: Bilingual Japanese On-site Marketing Coordinator ($1,000 Incentive* Potential)
Quality Team Leader
description:
I. **General Summary:**



Responsible for manufacturing and reporting tasks which include supporting team members in assigned manufacturing areas in the execution of the



group's responsibilities, as they operate according to customer specifications, following standard work instructions, and conveying parts between



processes. Overall responsibility for safety, quality of work, production and material flow of assigned team members.


**II. Essential Job Functions:**


A. Supporting production as a team member on the quality processes when necessary.



B. Ensure overtime coverage, when required, is communicated timely and that team members understand their assignments. Assist group leaders to



ensure overtime needs are covered, and the associated policy is consistently applied.



C. Follow standardized work procedures and work sequence for each process.



D. Maintain 5s and utilize TPS (Toyota Production System) techniques, as trained, to standardize process/methods to identify and eliminate waste, and



train, communicate, and support assigned team members regarding the same.



E. Work in a team environment on safety, quality, productivity and cost improvement projects.



F. Complete all working documentation, records and reports as requested. Participate in a manufacturing system and team environment on safety,



quality, productivity and cost improvement projects.



G. Understand all company policies and procedures.



H. Ensure team members follow and understand all standard work procedures associated with each task and all company policies.



I. Support assigned team members to meet production requirements and complete all production information sheets within the required timeframe.



J. Stop, call \& wait for all abnormal conditions according to TBA protocol and respond to assigned team members to support any Stop, call, wait from



them.



K. Follow all safety procedures and communicate issues with group leaders and assistant managers.



L. Review and understand previous shift problems, conduct pre\-shift checks, and support group leaders in meetings.



M. Follow, understand, and perform required quality checks according to standard work and Quality Alerts. Train, communicate, and support assigned



team members regarding the same.



N. Conducts audits as directed and responds to abnormalities.



O. Troubleshoot machines and follows escalation to group leader.



P. Understand and follow the TB Way philosophy within a team\-oriented environment, and train, communicate, and support assigned team members



regarding the same.



Q. Focus on employee relations centered on trust and teamwork.



R. Work on continually improving skills as outlined during evaluation.



S. Attend all required training as outlined on the training matrix.



T. Expected to be completely versed in assigned area and should be able to adequately instruct anyone to perform these duties.



U. Be proactive and reactive to resolve abnormalities in the process.



V. Use Kaizen (continuous improvement) and key performance indicators (KPI) in work areas for the opportunity to make the area or line efficient.



Monitor, update, and track KPI dashboard data.



W. Work towards achieving established group area targets based on direction from group leader.



X. Work beyond the regular schedule, which could include daily and weekend overtime.



Y. Follow safety quality, confidentiality, and information security policies and procedures



Z. Other duties as assigned.



AA. Follow safety, quality, confidentiality, and information security policies and procedures.



1\. Maintain training matrix for team leaders and/or team members, assuring training is effective and corrects sub\-standard performance.



2\. Maintain, plan, and prepare department instruction manuals.



3\. Ensure further development of team members through performance evaluation, development of training plans, feedback, coaching, and cascade



training.



AB. Collect, record, and analyze information while maintaining documentation regarding the activities in the assigned area as defined by company



policy, procedure, or practice.



1\. Prepare business reports and spread sheets of required data.



2\. Maintain specified records, files and logs of department/shift activities such as production, production problems, parts shortages, scrap, quality,



maintenance, etc. (manual and computer) as required. Perform other various administrative duties.



3\. Monitor and maintain team member time, attendance, and training records.



AC. Communicate frequently with internal contacts on various issues.



1\. Through daily communication meetings, keep team members informed on issues relevant to them and encourage two\-way communication.



2\. Maintain daily and/or frequent communication with upstream and downstream groups/areas and support departments to ensure efficient and



stable operations in a team\-based environment.



3\. Coordinate communications between shifts for assigned area(s).





***Functional (Quality Control)***
* Support the Quality Manager and overall plant quality system.


* Establish and maintain quality standards that meet or exceed customer requirements.


* Communicate customer or supplier quality issues to manufacturing, production control, and quality control departments.


* Initiate effective containment and investigation activities to prevent further quality problem/defect outflow in\-house and to customer.


* Effectively communicate with customers and/or suppliers to resolve quality problems.


* Investigate and countermeasure internal quality defects to improve plant processes.


* Ensure proper quality processes and procedures are followed and maintained plant wide.


* Work with all departments and all levels of management to promote quality.


**III. Minimum Qualifications/Requirements**:



**Education:*** High School Diploma or GED, preferred.


**Experience:**
* One (1\) to three (3\) years of experience relevant manufacturing experience is preferred.
* Previous leadership or supervisory experience preferred.


**Personal/Technical Skills:**
* General manufacturing and industrial equipment knowledge are preferred.
* Ability to use diverse hand tools and equipment.
* Ability to communicate and work effectively with multiple departments and all levels of the organization.
* Ability to work in a fast\-paced, multicultural work environment.
* Strong organization, time management, and planning skills and ability to manage multiple tasks with tight deadlines.
* Active listener with effective leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
* Ability to consistently show and maintain professionalism.
* Ability to work with a dynamic team and demanding customer(s).
* Strong customer service orientation.
* Ability to plan, direct, assign, and perform functions of assigned areas.
* Highly motivated, ambitious self\-starter.
* Ability to perform responsibilities with minimal supervision.
* Ability to communicate, document, and maintain records, procedures, and logs.
* Ability to be flexible and adapt to constantly changing production environment.
* Must be able to work a fixed or rotating shift schedule, including daily or weekend overtime.
* Ability to follow and implement plans to maintain safety, quality, 5S, cost, and efficiency for assigned areas.
* Thorough understanding of company products, trade terminology, manufacturing processes, and machine/equipment operation.
* Basic math and good communication skills.
* Strong attention to detail.
* Ability to analyze design proposals, diagnose issues, deliver effective feedback and suggested countermeasures.
* Ability to read, interpret, and blueprints, schematics, drawings, specifications, and technical instruction manuals.


**Language Skills:**
* Verbal and written communication skills in English.
* Japanese and/or Spanish is a plus.


**Computer/Software:**
* Working knowledge and experience with Microsoft Office and other computer\-based applications (e.g., MS Word, MS Excel, MS PowerPoint, Outlook, Internet, etc.).
* Previous experience working with integrated computer programs and/or applications. ERP System experience (e.g., Oracle) preferred.

**IV. Work Environment/Conditions:**





**Office:** N/A.





**Plant:** Standard automotive plant environment with moderate noise levels, flashing lights, alarms/notification systems, and sharp metal objects, etc. PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) such as safety glasses, steel toe shoes, hearing protection, heat protective gloves and sleeves, etc. may be required in engineering, manufacturing, or industrial areas. Personal attire standards may apply. Involves moderate or occasional presence of some slightly disagreeable conditions, i.e., cold, dust, heat, odor, etc.


**Travel:** N/A.


**V. Physical Demands:**



While performing the duties of this job, the team member is required to talk, hear, and see (with visual acuity). Our Sales Development team is the vanguard of our business, with Sales Development Reps (SDRs) driving interest in Absolute and initiating the sales cycle. In this role, you will collaborate with our Marketing and Account Executive teams to research and build a pipeline in new and existing accounts. You will follow up on both inbound and outbound leads across APAC and Japan (APJ).

Your exceptional communication and critical thinking skills will enable you to quickly assess opportunities and secure meetings with key account personas for our Account Executives. You’ll drive revenue growth from new account acquisitions and foster expansion and cross\-sell opportunities with existing customers.
**Responsibilities:*** Generate and qualify leads, identifying prospects interested in our cybersecurity solutions
* Prioritize and research prospects, understanding their current IT situation, challenges, and needs
* Engage prospective clients via phone, email, and social media (LinkedIn) to convey Absolute’s unique value propositions
* Schedule qualified meetings for Sales through outbound calling and strategic digital outreach, converting them into pipeline opportunities
* Build and prioritize strategic account lists aligned with sales team members within defined segments
* Foster alignment and positive partnerships with internal Field Sales, technical and Marketing teams
* Participate in virtual and/or in\-person events as needed
* Utilize our CRM system (Salesforce) to log activity and lead conversion data for business transparency
* Protect the security and privacy of Absolute and its customers

**Requirements:*** Both Japanese and English speaking required at a business professional level
* Resilience and a fearless approach to challenges, particularly in objection handling.
* Strong communication skills for effective interaction with customers, prospects, and team members
* Ambitious individual eager to learn and contribute significantly to the business's continued success
* Previous sales experience (1\-2 years preferred) with a proven track record of consistently opening qualified opportunities that lead to revenue
* Experience in B2B lead generation, ideally within the cybersecurity space, is a bonus but not essential

**Why Work For Us:**
You’re resilient and passionate about securing the Work from Anywhere era.  

So are we.
We’re in search of the best and the brightest – everyone from innovators, sellers and marketers to financers, operators and especially customer relationship managers – we’re looking for top tier talent to help us shape the next decade of security, drive innovation that enables customers with truly disruptive solutions and are dedicated to making a meaningful difference.
Headquartered in Seattle, Washington with international offices in Vancouver \- BC, Austin \- TX, Boulder \- CO, Ankeny – IA, Reading \- UK and Ho Chi Minh City – Vietnam, Absolute Security accelerates customers’ shift to work\-from\-anywhere through the industry’s first self\-healing Zero Trust platform, ensuring maximum security and uncompromised productivity. Only Absolute is embedded in more than half a billion devices, offering a permanent digital connection that intelligently and dynamically applies visibility, control and self\-healing capabilities to endpoints, applications, and network access to ensure their cyber resilience tailored for distributed workforces.
Our vision is to be the world’s most trusted security company – and to empower end users to connect securely and from anywhere, to all the applications they need to collaborate and get their work done, without interruptions and with an optimal network experience. We are looking for a proactive and detail\-oriented Recruitment Talent Sourcing Specialist to join our Talent Acquisition team. title: Engineer III, GT Project
description:
**Project Engineer III – Gas Turbine**

  


**Company Overview**

At Mitsubishi Power, we're not just building better clean energy technologies; we're architecting a better future. Our team is boldly redefining power generation to accelerate the world's energy transition. We operate as one team, pushing toward our vision of the future. We value problem solvers, prioritize collaboration, and support each other in an inclusive culture built on accountability and authenticity by demonstrating our values: Safety, Family, Innovative, Inclusive, Accountable \& Courageous. Together, we’re building the future we all aspire to \- making net zero a reality.  


**Role Overview**

The GT Project Engineer leads the engineering and design team, assuming overall responsibility for successful technical project execution. The GT Project Engineer acts as a liaison between the engineering team, project management and the customer with the responsibility for integrating the various engineering, scheduling, and purchasing aspect of the project in accordance with contract obligations. Familiarity with gas turbine and gas turbine auxiliaries design and construction is required (mechanical, electrical, and I\&C). Familiarity with Steam turbine, Generator, and BOP design and construction is desirable.
**Key Responsibilities**


* Demonstrates our core competencies: Action oriented, change champion, customer\-focused, developing self \& others, and ownership
* Serve as the primary technical point of contact for EPC partners, Owner’s Engineers, and Customers, interpreting and integrating design requirements from the customer or parent company in accordance with contract specifications.
* Lead planning, setup, and oversight of project engineering systems, including kickoffs, MDLs, DORs, contract deviation lists, and inter‑disciplinary workflows; coordinate engineering activities and resolve cross‑discipline issues.
* Translate contract requirements and scope of work into actionable engineering deliverables for discipline teams, ensuring contractual alignment.
* Monitor engineering schedules, budgets, and estimates through project completion, including periodic execution reviews, two‑week look‑ahead planning, expediting activities, escalation of action items, and preparation of technical status reports.
* Support Project Managers and Lead Project Engineers in tracking design changes, specification updates, ECNs, and contract deviations, including assessment of schedule, cost, and technical impacts.
* Conduct regular coordination meetings with international affiliated companies to support effective project execution and alignment.
* Review and comment on project documents and drawings to ensure compliance with contractual document flow and technical requirements.
* Support equipment specification, bid evaluation, vendor selection, and capacity assessments for MPWA‑sourced equipment on Gas Turbine and Combined Cycle projects.
* Maintain working knowledge of applicable engineering codes and standards for combustion turbines and auxiliary equipment, including ASME, ANSI, AWS, API, ASTM, NFPA, and NEC.
* Collaborate with Global Sourcing, Quality Assurance, and Project Management to coordinate vendors and subcontractors throughout project execution.
* Support Application Engineering in reviewing and evaluating customer specifications in coordination with the parent company, as required.
* Demonstrate strong multitasking and multidisciplinary communication skills across engineering, sourcing, and project teams.

**Requirements**


* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering required; Master’s Degree in Engineering preferred.
* 8\+ years of directly related technical experience in engineering or power project execution.
* Professional Engineer (P.E.) license preferred.
* Experience with SP3D and other 3D design tools such as SmartPlant or PDMS.
* Knowledge of ISO procedures and OSHA safety requirements.
* International business experience, including collaboration with global teams.
* Proficiency with project management and office tools, including MS Project, Excel, Word, Outlook, Visio, and technical diagrams/flowcharts.
* Strong technical interpretation skills, with the ability to analyze complex instructions presented in mathematical or diagrammatic form and manage both abstract and practical variables.
* Japanese and/or Spanish language fluency is a plus.
* Ability to travel to customer offices and construction sites

**Why Should You Apply?**


* Excellent Benefits (Medical, Dental, Vision \& 401K Matching).
* Excellent development programs and advancement opportunities.
* Tuition reimbursement and on\-the\-job training.
* Paid vacation, sick time, and holidays.
* Committed to quality products and services.
* Great working environment and culture.
* Employee Appreciation Programs and Event.

  



Mitsubishi Power is an Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) employer actively seeking to diversify the workforce and is committed to a policy of equal employment opportunity. Therefore, all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other legally recognized protected basis under applicable law, are strongly encouraged to apply.

title: Senior Systems Test Engineer, OEM TL
description:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self\-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride\-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider\-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15\+ U.S. states.


Waymo's Systems Engineering team works together to blend software and hardware systems in groundbreaking new ways. We set the high performance standards that ensure our vehicles run smoothly and keep passengers safe, then design and perform the tests that validate that performance. We're looking for talented teammates who’ll help us maintain strong teamwork and are passionate about driving results.


*This role follows a hybrid work schedule and reports to Systems Test Engineering Lead.*


**You will:**


* Helping partners develop test strategies for requirements, detailed test plans, scripts, and clear judgement criteria and acting as Waymo's test engineering representative across a number of technical domains
* Driving collaboration with test engineering stakeholders across partner organizations and identifying skill and organizational gaps within the OEM teams, as well as helping them build the expertise needed for cross\-system testing
* Spearhead collaboration with various engineering and leadership stakeholders across partner and Waymo organizations
* Guide partners in the transition from manual testing methodologies to modern, automated, and data\-driven approaches
* Ensure proper traceability and logging standards are met to facilitate seamless integration with Waymo’s central data repository
* Collaborate with internal infrastructure teams to design and prototype low\-latency solutions, accelerating the feedback loop by bridging the gap between partner test execution and internal reporting


**You have:**


* Strong background in systems engineering, test engineering, or automotive validation
* Demonstrated leadership experience with a track record of directly managing technical teams, providing mentorship, and driving engineering excellence
* Experience managing external partners or suppliers in a technical capacity
* Ability to navigate ambiguity and drive technical consensus across different organizational cultures
* Professional fluency in Korean or Japanese (written and verbal)


**We prefer:**


* Experience with requirements management tools (e.g., Jama, DOORS) and issue tracking (e.g., Jira)
* Experience with physical hands\-on testing of Autonomous Vehicles
* Strong knowledge of Systems Engineering principles and experience developing verification coverage plans from system level requirements
* Proficiency in Python or SQL for test automation or scripting


**Travel requirements**:


* Willingness to travel \>50% of the time or relocate to the partner sites


The expected base salary range for this full\-time position across US locations is listed below. Actual starting pay will be based on job\-related factors, including exact work location, experience, relevant training and education, and skill level. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the role location or, if the role can be performed remote, the specific salary range for your preferred location, during the hiring process.



Waymo employees are also eligible to participate in Waymo’s discretionary annual bonus program, equity incentive plan, and generous Company benefits program, subject to eligibility requirements.



Salary Range
$196,000—$248,000 USD

title: Associate Corporate Liaison
description:
Overview:

Serve as CEOs Liaison/Translator/Interpreter converting written (translation) and spoken (interpretation) information between English and Japanese, ensuring accuracy, tone, and context are maintained. Responsibilities include facilitating real\-time verbal communication, translating documents, managing terminology databases, and adhering to confidentiality protocols, typically requiring high fluency in these two languages. This liaison role must be detail\-oriented and have the ability to convert written materials from one language to another while preserving the original meaning, tone, and format. You will work with diverse documents.
Responsibilities:
* **Translation (Written)**: Translate documents (emails, reports, legal documents) while maintaining the original style, format, and technical terminology.
* **Interpretation (Spoken)**: Provide consecutive or simultaneous interpretation for meetings, conferences, interviews, or phone calls.
* **Accuracy \& Context:**Ensure precise conversion of messages, ensuring that cultural nuances are accurately communicated.
* **Confidentiality:**Strictly adhere to ethical standards and confidentiality agreements.
* **Quality Assurance:** Proofread and edit final translations for grammar, spelling, and punctuation.
* **Cultural Adaptation:** Ensure content is culturally sensitive and localized for the target region.
* **Fluency:**Near\-native proficiency in Japanese and English.
* **Technical Skills:** Proficiency with MS Office, translation software (CAT tools), and, for interpreters, familiarity with headset/microphone systems.
* **Communication:** Exceptional listening and speaking skills.
* **Soft Skills:** High levels of concentration, excellent listening and memory retention, cultural sensitivity, and the ability to work under pressure with tight deadlines.
* **Project Management:**Manage multiple deadlines and coordinate with internal teams to clarify project requirements.
* Support the CEO and his executive team by providing timely responses to requests for assistance. Build and maintain excellent relationships and communications with the Executive Team members, all staff and external contacts.
* Coordinate Noven / Hisamitsu weekly and monthly meeting logistics and prepare the meeting materials for dissemination.
* Coordinate Executive Team Staff Meeting materials are prepared and distributed to the appropriate parties. Ensure video conference / teleconference arrangements are set up prior to the start of the meeting and meeting materials are provided in a timely manner.
* Translate, prepare and distribute original correspondence requiring some technical knowledge for internal and external communication. Coordinate incoming departmental correspondence, determine priorities and route to the appropriate personnel.
* Manage and assist in the Budget process for the Office of the CEO.
* Review outgoing departmental correspondence to ensure accuracy and compliance with Company protocol; advise on corrective procedure as necessary.
* Accompany CEO during all business trips four times per year with a duration of 3 days each. Serve as his interpreter and translator
* Ensure project commitments made by CEO are understood and sufficiently staffed and developed.
* Exceptional attention to detail, strong time\-management, and high ethical standards regarding confidentiality


Qualifications:
**Competencies:**

* Language and Cultural Competence
* Professionalism and Ethical
* Commitment to execute specific instructions
* Cognitive Multitasking
* Executive Function skills:
	+ Active listening and comprehension
	+ Working memory skills
	+ Procedural awareness

 **Education / Experience:**


Proficient in English and Japanese both with verbal and written capabilities.


Bachelor’s Degree or five years or more professional experience at an executive administrator level. Technical education or specialized training in similar fields are preferred. Able to apply simple mathematics and formulas. PC literate with advanced proficiency in MS Office application software. Professional certification from the American Translators Association (ATA) or similar body, preferred.

 **Judgment / Decision Making:**


Duties performed independently toward general results; modification and interpretation of guidelines are often required. Establish priorities.

**Work Environment:**

Air\-conditioned office environment. Majority of time spent sitting at desk, operating computer and talking on the phone. Will be required to travel at least 4 times per year.

 **Physical Demands:**

Occasionally required to lift objects weighing up to twenty pounds. Visual and manual dexterity requires to operate computer and proofread documents.

title: Production Planner
description:
**Job purpose**


Coordinate the flow of work and material according to the production schedule to meet Customer Requirements while maintaining efficiency in the process.

**Duties and responsibilities**

* Prepare, Maintain, and Publish production packaging schedules to meet customer requirements while minimizing changeovers and managing potential overload.
* Create / maintain accurate process for computer modeling packaging and format schedules in a way that fully links to DNP\-Concord master production schedules.
* Publish the production plan at the agreed upon cadence.
* Revise production schedules as required due to design changes, labor or material shortages, backlog, demand shift, or other potential interruption while collaborating with involved parties to minimize impact. (i.e. Management, Customer Service, Sales, Production Leadership, etc.)
* Ensure capacity is available to meet or exceed plan based on labor and machine availability.
* Compile and prepare documentation related to production sequencing (Product Families) to minimize change and help drive efficiency on the floor. Create a uniform system to identify “like” items and batch manufacture when possible based on family.
* Compile KPI’s, such as production rate, progress to plan, material availability vs demand, Line or Machine change history, and customer score cards to present in Management and process meetings while maintaining historical files.
* Maintain records of original delivery request date / quantities, delivery commitments, and modifications.
* Communicate data accuracy concerns regarding master data for parts, subassemblies, and final assemblies within the ERP System (Including but not limited to: Material routing, Min / Max stock levels, safety stock, Lead Time, Method of Manufacturing, Yield, and Waste Factors.
* Create Bill of Material sequencing rules to drive uniformity across all Manufactured media.
* Work in conjunction with multiple departments to ensure the quality of goods, process efficiency, On time shipping, and communication of changes to upline management.
* Communicate with internal and external customers to confirm and coordinate shipping activities to resolve complaints or eliminate delays. Project, communicate and work to resolve likely late deliveries.
* Cross Train with production schedulers to ensure a deep understanding of the full converting process and serve as a backup for both Card and Photo Business.
* Scheduling and management of Work Orders in Epicor ERP to ensure accurate signals are sent to involved parties.
* For all the above: Find root cause of failures, recommend process improvements, and support efforts to improve processes.
* Support onsite transaction accuracy and year\-end inventory activity.
* Participate in projects and other activities as required by the manager.
* Other duties assigned by management.

**Qualifications**

* Preferred: Associate’s degree or equivalent experience.
* Knowledge of production control or inventory control software such as Epicor, etc.
* Three to five years of production control experience in manufacturing is desired.
* Power BI experience preferred.
* Strong computer skills and intermediate to advanced excel skills required
* Bilingual is a plus, Japanese preferred or link to existing or potential labor force

**Supervisory Responsibilities**


This role has no supervisory responsibilities.

**Physical Demands**

* Ability to work in an office setting, and spend primary working hours at a desk using a computer and other office equipment such as printers, copiers, postage machines, etc.
* While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to talk or hear. The employee frequently is required to sit, stand, and walk.. The employee is occasionally required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 10 pounds and infrequently lift and/or move up to 25 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, depth perception, peripheral vision, and ability to adjust focus  

***Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential functions.***
